content.content:
1ظْ# Architecture: Changelogue
     2ظْ
     3ظْ## Overview
     4ظْ
     5ظْChangelogue is an event-driven, hybrid-architecture system designed to centralize release discovery, automate validation, manage configurations, and distribute targeted notifications. It combines the high-concurrency performance of a Go-based polling engine with the reasoning capabilities of LLM-based SRE agents. By leveraging PostgreSQL for both persistent storage and message brokering, the system maintains high reliability and transactional consistency within a streamlined, single-binary deployment.
     6ظْ
     7ظْ## 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure
     8ظْ
     9ظْ* **Backend / Engine:** Go (Golang). Chosen for its lightweight concurrency model (Goroutines) to handle simultaneous polling across multiple registries.
    10ظْ* **Frontend / Dashboard:** Next.js (React) with Tailwind CSS. Provides a fast, modern control center for viewing release streams and managing configurations.
    11ظْ* **Database, Queue, & Event Bus:** PostgreSQL.
    12ظْ* *Persistent Storage:* Standard relational tables for release metadata and system configurations.
    13ظْ* *Task Queue:* Utilizes `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (via the **River** Go library) for robust, concurrent background job processing without race conditions.
    14ظْ* *Pub/Sub:* Utilizes native `LISTEN` and `NOTIFY` for real-time broadcasting to connected clients.
    15ظْ
    16ظْ
    17ظْ* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / OpenAI-compatible) orchestrated via [ADK-Go](https://google.github.io/adk-go/) v0.5.0 for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous release evaluation.
    18ظْ* **Packaging:** Single binary deployment utilizing Go's `//go:embed` to serve the Next.js static export directly from the Go server.
    19ظْ
    20ظْ## 2. System Design & Abstractions
    21ظْ
    22ظْThe system is decoupled into four primary layers communicating entirely through PostgreSQL using the Transactional Outbox pattern:
    23ظْ
    24ظْ1. **Ingestion Layer** ظ¤ Polling workers and webhook handlers discover new releases from upstream registries.
    25ظْ2. **Notification Routing** ظ¤ River workers send immediate notifications to source-level subscribers on new releases.
    26ظْ3. **Agent Layer** ظ¤ ADK-Go agents research releases, consult context sources, and produce semantic release reports.
    27ظْ4. **Routing & Output** ظ¤ Notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks) deliver alerts via `Sender`.
    28ظْ
    29ظْA **Project** is the central domain entity ظ¤ it groups multiple ingestion sources, context sources, and notification subscriptions under a single tracked piece of software.
    30ظْ
    31ظْ```mermaid
    32ظْgraph LR
    33ظْ    %% Data Flow
    34ظْ    subgraph Dashboard [API & Dashboard]
    35ظْ        API[REST API] --> |CRUD| PG
    36ظْ        API --> |SSE| Browser[Next.js Dashboard]
    37ظْ    end
    38ظْ
    39ظْ    subgraph Ingestion [Ingestion Layer]
    40ظْ        I_Hub(Docker Hub Poller) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    41ظْ        I_Git(GitHub Atom Feed) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    42ظْ    end
    43ظْ
    44ظْ    subgraph Core [Event Bus & Queue - PostgreSQL]
    45ظْ        Engine((Go Engine)) --> |INSERT release + jobs\nTransactional Outbox| PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    46ظْ    end
    47ظْ
    48ظْ    subgraph Notification [Notification Routing]
    49ظْ        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver notify_release| NotifyWorker[Notify Worker]
    50ظْ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Slack(Slack)
    51ظْ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Discord(Discord)
    52ظْ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Email(Email)
    53ظْ        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Hook(Webhooks)
    54ظْ    end
    55ظْ
    56ظْ    subgraph AgentLayer [Agent Layer]
    57ظْ        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver agent_run| AgentWorker[Agent Worker]
    58ظْ        AgentWorker --> |ADK-Go| LLM[LLM Provider]
    59ظْ        AgentWorker --> |Research| ContextSources[Context Sources]
    60ظْ        AgentWorker --> |Produce| SemanticRelease[Semantic Release + Report]
    61ظْ    end
    62ظْ
    63ظْ```
    64ظْ
    65ظْ### Entity Relationship Model
    66ظْ
    67ظْ```mermaid
    68ظْerDiagram
    69ظْ    projects ||--o{ sources : "has many"
    70ظْ    projects ||--o{ context_sources : "has many"
    71ظْ    projects ||--o{ semantic_releases : "has many"
    72ظْ    projects ||--o{ agent_runs : "has many"
    73ظْ    sources ||--o{ releases : "produces"
    74ظْ    subscriptions }o--|| notification_channels : "routes to"
    75ظْ    subscriptions }o--o| sources : "source-level"
    76ظْ    subscriptions }o--o| projects : "project-level"
    77ظْ    semantic_releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "composed of"
    78ظْ    releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "contributes to"
    79ظْ
    80ظْ    projects {
    81ظْ        uuid id PK
    82ظْ        string name UK
    83ظْ        text agent_prompt
    84ظْ        jsonb agent_rules
    85ظْ    }
    86ظْ    sources {
    87ظْ        uuid id PK
    88ظْ        uuid project_id FK
    89ظْ        string provider
    90ظْ        string repository UK
    91ظْ        boolean enabled
    92ظْ    }
    93ظْ    releases {
    94ظْ        uuid id PK
    95ظْ        uuid source_id FK
    96ظْ        string version
    97ظْ        jsonb raw_data
    98ظْ    }
    99ظْ    semantic_releases {
   100ظْ        uuid id PK
   101ظْ        uuid project_id FK
   102ظْ        string version
   103ظْ        jsonb report
   104ظْ        string status
   105ظْ    }
   106ظْ    agent_runs {
   107ظْ        uuid id PK
   108ظْ        uuid project_id FK
   109ظْ        uuid semantic_release_id FK
   110ظْ        string trigger
   111ظْ        string status
   112ظْ    }
   113ظْ    subscriptions {
   114ظْ        uuid id PK
   115ظْ        uuid channel_id FK
   116ظْ        string type
   117ظْ        uuid source_id FK
   118ظْ        uuid project_id FK
   119ظْ    }
   120ظْ    notification_channels {
   121ظْ        uuid id PK
   122ظْ        string type
   123ظْ        string name
   124ظْ        jsonb config
   125ظْ    }
   126ظْ```
   127ظْ
   128ظْ### 2.1 The Event-Driven Backbone (PostgreSQL Only)
   129ظْ
   130ظْComponents do not call each other synchronously. Instead, they rely on PostgreSQL to guarantee delivery:
   131ظْ
   132ظْ* **The Transactional Outbox:** When a new release is detected, the ingestion worker writes the metadata to the `releases` table (linked to its `source_id`) and simultaneously enqueues River jobs (notification and/or agent) *within the exact same SQL transaction*. This guarantees no events are ever lost.
   133ظْ* **Real-time Pub/Sub:** Database triggers use `pg_notify` to broadcast lightweight events (e.g., telling the Next.js UI via SSE to refresh) over standard Postgres connections using `LISTEN`.
   134ظْ* **Reliable Queues:** River workers process jobs using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, ensuring exactly-once processing for both notification delivery and agent runs.
   135ظْ* **Project-Centric Model:** All data flows through the `projects` ظْ `sources` ظْ `releases` hierarchy. Subscriptions can attach at the source level (for raw release notifications) or the project level (for semantic release notifications).
   136ظْ
   137ظْ### 2.2 Provider Interfaces (I/O)
   138ظْ
   139ظْAll external integrations are abstracted behind strict Go interfaces.
   140ظْ
   141ظْ* `IIngestionSource`: Standardizes how polling workers fetch data. Adding a new registry (like npm or NuGet) only requires implementing this interface. Each implementation maps to a `source_type` in the database.
   142ظْ* `Sender`: Standardizes output routing. Each implementation maps to a `type` in the `notification_channels` table (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   143ظْ
   144ظْ### 2.3 REST API & Dashboard
   145ظْ
   146ظْThe Go server exposes a RESTful API (`/api/v1`) serving the Next.js dashboard and external consumers:
   147ظْ
   148ظْ* **Resource CRUD:** Projects, sources, subscriptions, notification channels, context sources ظ¤ all manageable through the API.
   149ظْ* **Read-Only Releases:** Releases and semantic releases are queryable but not directly writable via the API ظ¤ they're created exclusively through the ingestion layer and agent runs.
   150ظْ* **Source Operations:** Manual poll trigger (`POST /sources/{id}/poll`) and channel test notifications (`POST /channels/{id}/test`).
   151ظْ* **SSE Real-Time Events:** `GET /api/v1/events` streams server-sent events backed by PostgreSQL `LISTEN/NOTIFY`, pushing release and semantic release updates to connected dashboard clients.
   152ظْ* **API Key Auth:** Bearer token authentication with hashed key storage. `NO_AUTH=true` disables auth for development.
   153ظْ* **Rate Limiting:** Per-key token bucket (10 rps, 20 burst) with standard `X-Ratelimit-*` response headers.
   154ظْ* **Stats & Trends:** Dashboard stats and time-bucketed release count trends via `/stats` and `/stats/trend`.
   155ظْ
   156ظْSee [API.md](API.md) for the full endpoint reference.
   157ظْ
   158ظْ### 2.4 Notification Routing
   159ظْ
   160ظْWhen a new source release is detected, a `notify_release` River job is enqueued in the same transaction as the release insert. The notification worker:
   161ظْ
   162ظْ1. Resolves all source-level subscriptions for the release's source.
   163ظْ2. For each subscription, sends a notification to the linked channel via `Sender`.
   164ظْ3. Checks the project's agent rules ظ¤ if the release matches (major bump, security patch, version pattern), enqueues an `agent_run` job automatically.
   165ظْ
   166ظْ### 2.5 Agent Layer (Implemented)
   167ظْ
   168ظْFor project-level intelligence, an `agent_run` River job triggers an LLM agent (via ADK-Go v0.5.0) that:
   169ظْ
   170ظْ1. Gathers recent source releases for the project using the `get_releases` tool.
   171ظْ2. Inspects individual release details (changelogs, raw data) using `get_release_detail`.
   172ظْ3. Consults context sources (runbooks, docs, monitoring dashboards) using `list_context_sources`.
   173ظْ4. Produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured `SemanticReport` (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, download commands, urgency, recommendation).
   174ظْ
   175ظْAgent behavior is configured per-project via `agent_prompt` (custom instructions) and `agent_rules` (structured triggers like `on_major_release`, `on_security_patch`, `version_pattern`, `wait_for_all_sources`). Agent runs are tracked in the `agent_runs` table for observability and auditability.
   176ظْ
   177ظْ**LLM Provider Support:**
   178ظْ
   179ظْThe agent supports multiple LLM backends via the `LLM_PROVIDER` environment variable:
   180ظْ* **Gemini** (default) ظ¤ Uses `GOOGLE_API_KEY` with ADK-Go's native Gemini model integration.
   181ظْ* **OpenAI** ظ¤ Uses `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `OPENAI_BASE_URL` via an OpenAI-compatible adapter (`internal/agent/openai/`).
   182ظْ
   183ظْ**Implementation details (`internal/agent/`):**
   184ظْ
   185ظْ* **`tools.go`** ظ¤ Three ADK function tools (`get_releases`, `get_release_detail`, `list_context_sources`) created via `functiontool.New()`. Tools are scoped to a project via `toolFactory` which holds the `AgentDataStore` and project ID.
   186ظْ* **`orchestrator.go`** ظ¤ `Orchestrator` manages the full agent lifecycle: loading project config, creating an ADK `llmagent` with the configured LLM backend, running the agent via `runner.Runner`, parsing the JSON report, and persisting the `SemanticRelease` in a transaction.
   187ظْ* **`worker.go`** ظ¤ `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`, loading the agent run from the store and delegating to `Orchestrator.RunAgent()`.
   188ظْ* **Graceful degradation** ظ¤ If no LLM API key is set, the orchestrator is not created, the worker is not registered, and a warning is logged. The rest of the system continues to function. Agent jobs remain in the River queue until the key is configured.
   189ظْ
   190ظْ### 2.6 Agentic Tooling (Planned: SRE Validation)
   191ظْ
   192ظْFor deep validation, Changelogue will utilize SRE agents with a suite of abstracted tools:
   193ظْ
   194ظْ* `UpgradeBaseABoxConfig(version)`
   195ظْ* `CheckAgentStatus(environment)`
   196ظْ* `SyncOpsOpsack(payload)`
   197ظْThis will allow the agent to autonomously deploy a sandbox, verify that the deployment is healthy, and rollback or alert on failure.
   198ظْ
   199ظْ## 3. Data Flow: Lifecycle of a Release Event
   200ظْ
   201ظْ1. **Discovery:** A Go worker polling Docker Hub detects a new base image tag for a configured source (e.g., the "Docker Hub" source under the "Go Runtime" project). Source-level exclusion filters (`exclude_version_regexp`, `exclude_prereleases`) are applied ظ¤ filtered versions are discarded immediately.
   202ظْ2. **Ingestion & Transaction:** The worker stores the raw upstream payload and executes a single database transaction to insert the record into `releases` (linked to its `source_id`) and enqueue a `notify_release` River job.
   203ظْ3. **Notification (Source-Level):** A River worker picks up the `notify_release` job, resolves source-level subscriptions, and sends alerts to configured notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   204ظْ4. **Agent Rule Check:** The same notification worker evaluates the project's `agent_rules` against the new release version. If criteria match (major bump, security patch, version pattern), it enqueues an `agent_run` River job.
   205ظْ5. **Agent Analysis (Project-Level):** A River worker picks up the `agent_run` job. The ADK-Go agent gathers recent releases, consults context sources, and produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured report (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, urgency, recommendation).
   206ظْ6. **Broadcast:** PostgreSQL triggers fire `NOTIFY` payloads on release insert and semantic release completion, pushing SSE events to connected dashboard clients.
   207ظْ7. **Project-Level Notification:** When a semantic release is completed, project-level subscribers are notified with the AI-generated report.
   208ظْ
   209ظْ## 4. Directory Structure
   210ظْ
   211ظْ```text
   212ظْ/changelogue
   213ظْظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ cmd/
   214ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ server/          # Main Go application entry point
   215ظْظ¤é   ظ¤¤ظ¤ظ¤ agent/           # Agent CLI ظ¤ run agent analysis for a project
   216ظْظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ internal/
   217ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ api/             # REST API handlers, middleware, SSE broadcaster
   218ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ ingestion/       # Polling workers (IIngestionSource: Docker Hub, GitHub Atom)
   219ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ agent/           # ADK-Go agent for semantic release analysis
   220ظْظ¤é   ظ¤é   ظ¤¤ظ¤ظ¤ openai/      # OpenAI-compatible LLM provider adapter
   221ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ routing/         # Notification routing worker and channel implementations (Sender)
   222ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ queue/           # River queue job definitions (NotifyJobArgs, AgentJobArgs)
   223ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ db/              # Connection pool and schema migrations
   224ظْظ¤é   ظ¤¤ظ¤ظ¤ models/          # Shared domain structs (Release, Project, SemanticRelease, etc.)
   225ظْظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ web/                 # Next.js frontend application
   226ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ app/             # App Router pages (dashboard, projects, releases, channels, etc.)
   227ظْظ¤é   ظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ components/      # React components organized by domain
   228ظْظ¤é   ظ¤¤ظ¤ظ¤ lib/             # Client utilities and API wrappers
   229ظْظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ scripts/             # Integration test harness
   230ظْظ¤£ظ¤ظ¤ docs/                # Design documents and plans
   231ظْظ¤¤ظ¤ظ¤ go.mod
   232ظْ```

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

data.prompt:
You are a spec document reviewer. Review the design spec at /Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-16-github-oauth-login-design.md for completeness, correctness, and potential issues.

Context: This is a GitHub OAuth login feature for a Go backend + Next.js static export frontend app called Changelogue. The Go backend serves the static frontend via go:embed. Current auth is API key-based (Bearer tokens). The spec adds GitHub OAuth with server-side sessions stored in PostgreSQL and HttpOnly cookies.

Key project facts:
- Go 1.25 backend, PostgreSQL, River job queue
- Next.js static export embedded in Go binary
- Existing API key auth in internal/api/auth.go
- Migrations run at startup via internal/db/migrations.go
- NO_AUTH=true disables auth in dev

Review for:
1. Security issues (CSRF, cookie security, token handling, state validation)
2. Missing edge cases (what if both allowlists are empty? what if GitHub API fails?)
3. Schema issues (missing indexes, type choices)
4. Flow gaps (what happens on session expiry mid-use?)
5. Consistency with existing codebase patterns
6. Ambiguities that would block implementation

Format your response as:
- APPROVED (if no blocking issues)
- ISSUES FOUND (list each issue with severity: BLOCKING or SUGGESTION)

Also read the project's ARCH.md and DESIGN.md to verify consistency.

content.content:
1ظْ# Design Document: Changelogue
     2ظْ
     3ظْ## 1. System Context & Principles
     4ظْ
     5ظْ**Changelogue** is designed to solve the "release noise" problem by acting as an intelligent middleware between upstream software registries (Docker Hub, GitHub) and downstream operational systems (Base A Box, Ops Opsack, Slack).
     6ظْ
     7ظْ### Core Design Principles
     8ظْ
     9ظْ* **Single Binary Deployment:** The entire system (Go backend, Next.js frontend frontend, and background workers) compiles down to a single executable.
    10ظْ* **Zero-Loss Eventing:** Utilizing the Transactional Outbox pattern with PostgreSQL ensures no webhook or polled release is ever lost, even during system restarts.
    11ظْ* **Abstracted Intelligence:** The LLM reasoning and agentic validation are isolated from the core ingestion loops, allowing the system to fall back to basic regex routing if the LLM API is unavailable.
    12ظْ
    13ظْ## 2. Component Design & Interactions
    14ظْ
    15ظْ### 2.1 Core Models
    16ظْ
    17ظْThe system uses a set of Go structs that map directly to the database schema. These models replace the previous `ReleaseEvent` IR approach -- instead of normalizing into an intermediate representation, raw provider data is stored as JSONB and analyzed by AI agents.
    18ظْ
    19ظْ#### Release
    20ظْ
    21ظْThe primary release representation. Stores the raw provider payload as JSONB in `RawData`, preserving full fidelity from the upstream source. Replaces the old `ReleaseEvent` IR.
    22ظْ
    23ظْ```go
    24ظْtype Release struct {
    25ظْ    ID         string          `json:"id"`                    // UUID
    26ظْ    SourceID   string          `json:"source_id"`             // FK to sources table
    27ظْ    Version    string          `json:"version"`               // e.g., "v1.21.0-rc.1"
    28ظْ    RawData    json.RawMessage `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`    // Full upstream payload as JSONB
    29ظْ    ReleasedAt *time.Time      `json:"released_at,omitempty"` // Upstream release timestamp
    30ظْ    CreatedAt  time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
    31ظْ}
    32ظْ```
    33ظْ
    34ظْ#### ContextSource
    35ظْ
    36ظْRead-only references that agents consult during analysis (runbooks, deployment docs, monitoring dashboards). These are not polled -- they provide background context for generating richer semantic release reports.
    37ظْ
    38ظْ```go
    39ظْtype ContextSource struct {
    40ظْ    ID        string          `json:"id"`         // UUID
    41ظْ    ProjectID string          `json:"project_id"` // FK to projects table
    42ظْ    Type      string          `json:"type"`       // 'url', 'github_repo', 'confluence', etc.
    43ظْ    Name      string          `json:"name"`
    44ظْ    Config    json.RawMessage `json:"config"`     // Type-specific config (URL, credentials ref, etc.)
    45ظْ    CreatedAt time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
    46ظْ    UpdatedAt time.Time       `json:"updated_at"`
    47ظْ}
    48ظْ```
    49ظْ
    50ظْ#### SemanticRelease & SemanticReport
    51ظْ
    52ظْAI-generated, project-level analysis. An agent correlates one or more source-level releases into a single semantic release with a structured report. Replaces the old pipeline node-by-node result accumulation.
    53ظْ
    54ظْ```go
    55ظْtype SemanticReport struct {
    56ظْ    Summary        string `json:"summary"`        // Human-readable summary of the release
    57ظْ    Availability   string `json:"availability"`   // Artifact availability status
    58ظْ    Adoption       string `json:"adoption"`       // Network/ecosystem adoption metrics
    59ظْ    Urgency        string `json:"urgency"`        // LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / CRITICAL
    60ظْ    Recommendation string `json:"recommendation"` // Agent's recommended action
    61ظْ}
    62ظْ
    63ظْtype SemanticRelease struct {
    64ظْ    ID          string          `json:"id"`                      // UUID
    65ظْ    ProjectID   string          `json:"project_id"`              // FK to projects table
    66ظْ    Version     string          `json:"version"`
    67ظْ    Report      json.RawMessage `json:"report,omitempty"`        // SemanticReport as JSONB
    68ظْ    Status      string          `json:"status"`                  // pending, processing, completed, failed
    69ظْ    Error       string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
    70ظْ    CreatedAt   time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
    71ظْ    CompletedAt *time.Time      `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
    72ظْ}
    73ظْ```
    74ظْ
    75ظْ#### AgentRun
    76ظْ
    77ظْProvides observability into agent executions. Each run is scoped to a project and optionally linked to a semantic release. Captures the exact prompt for debugging and auditability.
    78ظْ
    79ظْ```go
    80ظْtype AgentRun struct {
    81ظْ    ID                string     `json:"id"`                              // UUID
    82ظْ    ProjectID         string     `json:"project_id"`                      // FK to projects table
    83ظْ    SemanticReleaseID *string    `json:"semantic_release_id,omitempty"`   // FK to semantic_releases
    84ظْ    Trigger           string     `json:"trigger"`                         // 'release', 'manual', etc.
    85ظْ    Status            string     `json:"status"`                          // pending, running, completed, failed
    86ظْ    PromptUsed        string     `json:"prompt_used,omitempty"`           // Exact prompt sent to LLM
    87ظْ    Error             string     `json:"error,omitempty"`
    88ظْ    StartedAt         *time.Time `json:"started_at,omitempty"`
    89ظْ    CompletedAt       *time.Time `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
    90ظْ    CreatedAt         time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
    91ظْ}
    92ظْ```
    93ظْ
    94ظْ#### AgentRules
    95ظْ
    96ظْStructured rules governing when and how an agent should analyze releases for a project. Stored as JSONB in the `projects.agent_rules` column.
    97ظْ
    98ظْ```go
    99ظْtype AgentRules struct {
   100ظْ    OnMajorRelease  bool   `json:"on_major_release,omitempty"`  // Trigger on major version bumps
   101ظْ    OnMinorRelease  bool   `json:"on_minor_release,omitempty"`  // Trigger on minor version bumps
   102ظْ    OnSecurityPatch bool   `json:"on_security_patch,omitempty"` // Trigger on security patches
   103ظْ    VersionPattern  string `json:"version_pattern,omitempty"`   // Regex for custom version matching
   104ظْ}
   105ظْ```
   106ظْ
   107ظْ### 2.2 Processing Architecture (Agent + Notification Routing)
   108ظْ
   109ظْThe configurable processing pipeline has been replaced by an agent-driven architecture with two distinct paths:
   110ظْ
   111ظْ1. **Notification Routing** -- When a new source release is detected, a `NotifyJobArgs` River job is enqueued. The notification worker resolves source-level subscriptions and sends alerts to configured channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks). This provides immediate, low-latency notifications without waiting for AI analysis.
   112ظْ
   113ظْ2. **Agent Layer** -- For project-level intelligence, an `AgentJobArgs` River job triggers an LLM agent (via ADK-Go) that researches releases, consults context sources, and produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured `SemanticReport`. Agent behavior is configured per-project via `agent_prompt` and `agent_rules`. Detailed design for both paths will be specified in later tasks (Phase 2: Notification, Phase 3: Agent).
   114ظْ
   115ظْ### 2.3 SRE Agent Orchestration (The Validation Sandbox)
   116ظْ
   117ظْFor high-urgency releases or critical internal projects (like the token compiler), Changelogue employs an SRE agent to validate the build.
   118ظْ
   119ظْTo maintain predictable execution, the agent is modeled as a state machine (suitable for implementation via frameworks like LangGraph or the Claude Agent SDK).
   120ظْ
   121ظْ**Agent Toolset:**
   122ظْ
   123ظْ* `GetBaseABoxConfig(repo)`: Fetches the current master config.
   124ظْ* `DraftConfigUpgrade(repo, newVersion)`: Proposes a new configuration.
   125ظْ* `DeploySandbox(config)`: Triggers a test deployment.
   126ظْ* `QueryAgentStatus(envId)`: Checks the health of the newly deployed agent.
   127ظْ
   128ظْ**Agent Workflow Loop:**
   129ظْ
   130ظْ1. **Observe:** Agent receives the `ReleaseEvent` IR and the "Production Record" summary.
   131ظْ2. **Act:** Agent uses `DraftConfigUpgrade` and `DeploySandbox`.
   132ظْ3. **Evaluate:** Agent uses `QueryAgentStatus` to ensure no regressions occurred (e.g., login failures).
   133ظْ4. **Resolve:** If healthy, the agent approves the release. If degraded, it formats a critical error summary for the Notification Matrix.
   134ظْ
   135ظْ## 3. Database Schema (PostgreSQL)
   136ظْ
   137ظْThe system relies on ten core tables to manage state, configuration, agent intelligence, and notification routing. All primary keys are UUIDs generated by PostgreSQL's `gen_random_uuid()`.
   138ظْ
   139ظْ```sql
   140ظْ-- Tracked software projects (the central entity)
   141ظْCREATE TABLE projects (
   142ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   143ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,          -- display name: 'Go Runtime', 'PostgreSQL'
   144ظْ    description TEXT,
   145ظْ    agent_prompt TEXT,                           -- custom prompt for agent analysis of this project
   146ظْ    agent_rules JSONB,                          -- structured rules governing agent behavior
   147ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   148ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   149ظْ);
   150ظْ
   151ظْ-- Configured ingestion sources (polling-based: GitHub, Docker Hub)
   152ظْ-- A project can have multiple sources (e.g., GitHub + Docker Hub)
   153ظْCREATE TABLE sources (
   154ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   155ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   156ظْ    provider VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,              -- 'dockerhub', 'github'
   157ظْ    repository VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
   158ظْ    poll_interval_seconds INT DEFAULT 900,
   159ظْ    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
   160ظْ    config JSONB,                               -- provider-specific config (exclude patterns, etc.)
   161ظْ    last_polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   162ظْ    last_error TEXT,
   163ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   164ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   165ظْ    UNIQUE(provider, repository)                -- no duplicate provider+repo pairs
   166ظْ);
   167ظْ
   168ظْ-- Context sources (read-only references for agent research)
   169ظْ-- Examples: runbooks, deployment docs, monitoring dashboards
   170ظْCREATE TABLE context_sources (
   171ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   172ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   173ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,                  -- 'url', 'github_repo', 'confluence', etc.
   174ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   175ظْ    config JSONB NOT NULL,                      -- type-specific config (URL, credentials ref, etc.)
   176ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   177ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   178ظْ);
   179ظْ
   180ظْ-- Source-level releases (detected from polling sources)
   181ظْ-- References the source it came from; project is reachable via JOIN
   182ظْCREATE TABLE releases (
   183ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   184ظْ    source_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   185ظْ    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   186ظْ    raw_data JSONB,                             -- upstream payload as-is
   187ظْ    released_at TIMESTAMPTZ,                    -- when the upstream released it
   188ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   189ظْ    UNIQUE(source_id, version)                  -- idempotent: same version from same source
   190ظْ);
   191ظْ
   192ظْ-- Project-level semantic releases (AI-generated, correlating source releases)
   193ظْ-- An agent analyzes source releases and produces a semantic release with a report
   194ظْCREATE TABLE semantic_releases (
   195ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   196ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   197ظْ    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   198ظْ    report JSONB,                               -- AI-generated analysis report
   199ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending, processing, completed, failed
   200ظْ    error TEXT,
   201ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   202ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   203ظْ    UNIQUE(project_id, version)
   204ظْ);
   205ظْ
   206ظْ-- Join table: which source releases compose a semantic release
   207ظْCREATE TABLE semantic_release_sources (
   208ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   209ظْ    release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   210ظْ    PRIMARY KEY (semantic_release_id, release_id)
   211ظْ);
   212ظْ
   213ظْ-- Notification channels (standalone, reusable across subscriptions)
   214ظْCREATE TABLE notification_channels (
   215ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   216ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   217ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,                  -- 'slack', 'pagerduty', 'webhook', 'email'
   218ظْ    config JSONB NOT NULL,                      -- webhook_url, channel_id, routing_key, etc.
   219ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   220ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   221ظْ);
   222ظْ
   223ظْ-- Subscriptions: two types (source-level and project-level)
   224ظْ-- Source subscriptions notify on raw releases; project subscriptions notify on semantic releases
   225ظْCREATE TABLE subscriptions (
   226ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   227ظْ    channel_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES notification_channels(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   228ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('source', 'project')),
   229ظْ    source_id UUID REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   230ظْ    project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   231ظْ    version_filter TEXT,                        -- regex: only notify for matching versions
   232ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   233ظْ    CHECK (
   234ظْ        (type = 'source'  AND source_id  IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   235ظْ        (type = 'project' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id  IS NULL)
   236ظْ    )
   237ظْ);
   238ظْ
   239ظْ-- Agent runs (scoped to project, tracks each agent execution)
   240ظْCREATE TABLE agent_runs (
   241ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   242ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   243ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   244ظْ    trigger VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,              -- what initiated the run: 'release', 'manual', etc.
   245ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- pending, running, completed, failed
   246ظْ    prompt_used TEXT,                            -- the actual prompt sent to the LLM
   247ظْ    error TEXT,
   248ظْ    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   249ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   250ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   251ظْ);
   252ظْ
   253ظْ-- API authentication keys
   254ظْCREATE TABLE api_keys (
   255ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   256ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   257ظْ    key_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE,       -- SHA-256 hash, raw key shown once
   258ظْ    key_prefix VARCHAR(12) NOT NULL,            -- 'rg_live_' or 'rg_test_' prefix for identification
   259ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   260ظْ    last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   261ظْ);
   262ظْ```
   263ظْ
   264ظْ### SSE Triggers
   265ظْ
   266ظْTwo PostgreSQL triggers publish real-time events via `pg_notify` on the `release_events` channel:
   267ظْ
   268ظْ1. **`release_created_trigger`** ظ¤ Fires on `INSERT` into `releases`. Publishes `{"type": "release", "id": "<uuid>"}`.
   269ظْ2. **`semantic_release_trigger`** ظ¤ Fires on `INSERT OR UPDATE` on `semantic_releases` when `status` transitions to `'completed'`. Publishes `{"type": "semantic_release", "id": "<uuid>"}`.
   270ظْ
   271ظْ### 3.1 Schema Design Notes
   272ظْ
   273ظْ* **Projects as the central entity:** A project represents a tracked piece of software (e.g., "Go Runtime"). It can have multiple sources ظ¤ a GitHub source for repos you own and a Docker Hub source for polling the container image. The `agent_prompt` and `agent_rules` fields configure how the AI agent analyzes releases for this project, replacing the old `pipeline_config` approach with flexible, agent-driven intelligence.
   274ظْ* **Multi-source ingestion:** The `sources` table uses `UNIQUE(provider, repository)` instead of `UNIQUE(repository)` ظ¤ the same repository name on different providers won't collide. Each source belongs to exactly one project via `project_id`. Provider-specific settings (exclude patterns, pre-release filtering) live in the `config` JSONB column.
   275ظْ* **Context sources:** The `context_sources` table stores read-only references (URLs, repo links, documentation) that the agent can consult during analysis. These are not polled ظ¤ they provide background context for generating richer semantic release reports.
   276ظْ* **Release provenance:** Releases reference `source_id` (not raw strings), so you always know which source detected a release. The unique constraint `UNIQUE(source_id, version)` allows the same version to exist from different sources (e.g., Go 1.21.0 from GitHub and Docker Hub are separate release records).
   277ظْ* **Semantic releases:** The `semantic_releases` table captures AI-generated, project-level analysis. An agent correlates one or more source-level releases (via the `semantic_release_sources` join table) into a single semantic release with a structured report. This replaces the old `pipeline_jobs` table ظ¤ instead of tracking node-by-node pipeline progress, the system tracks agent-driven analysis runs.
   278ظْ* **Agent runs:** The `agent_runs` table provides observability into agent executions. Each run is scoped to a project and optionally linked to a semantic release. The `trigger` field records what initiated the run, and `prompt_used` captures the exact prompt for debugging and auditability.
   279ظْ* **Subscription routing:** Subscriptions now support two granularity levels via a `type` check constraint: `'source'` subscriptions (linked to a specific source via `source_id`) notify on raw releases, while `'project'` subscriptions (linked via `project_id`) notify on semantic releases. This replaces the old project-only subscription model.
   280ظْ* **Notification channels:** Separating channel registration from subscription routing allows multiple subscriptions to reference the same Slack channel or PagerDuty service. The `config` JSONB column stores provider-specific credentials (webhook URLs, routing keys).
   281ظْ
   282ظْ## 4. Notification Routing
   283ظْ
   284ظْ### 4.1 Overview
   285ظْ
   286ظْWhen the ingestion layer detects a new release, it enqueues a `NotifyJobArgs` River job in the same transaction that inserts the release (transactional outbox pattern). The notification routing system picks up these jobs and delivers alerts to all source-level subscribers.
   287ظْ
   288ظْ### 4.2 Sender Interface
   289ظْ
   290ظْAll notification channel types implement the `Sender` interface:
   291ظْ
   292ظْ```go
   293ظْtype Sender interface {
   294ظْ    Send(ctx context.Context, ch *models.NotificationChannel, msg Notification) error
   295ظْ}
   296ظْ```
   297ظْ
   298ظْThe `Notification` struct is the channel-agnostic payload:
   299ظْ
   300ظْ```go
   301ظْtype Notification struct {
   302ظْ    Title   string `json:"title"`
   303ظْ    Body    string `json:"body"`
   304ظْ    Version string `json:"version"`
   305ظْ}
   306ظْ```
   307ظْ
   308ظْ### 4.3 Channel Types
   309ظْ
   310ظْ| Type | Implementation | Config Fields | Payload Format |
   311ظْ|------|---------------|---------------|----------------|
   312ظْ| `webhook` | `WebhookSender` | `{"url": "..."}` | Raw JSON `Notification` |
   313ظْ| `slack` | `SlackSender` | `{"webhook_url": "..."}` | Slack Block Kit (header + section) |
   314ظْ| `discord` | `DiscordSender` | `{"webhook_url": "..."}` | Discord webhook with embeds |
   315ظْ| `email` | `EmailSender` | `{"smtp_host", "smtp_port", "username", "password", "from_address", "to_addresses"}` | Multipart MIME (HTML + plain text) via SMTP |
   316ظْ
   317ظْEach sender parses provider-specific config from the channel's `Config` JSONB field, formats the notification into the provider's expected payload structure, and delivers via the appropriate transport. HTTP-based senders (webhook, Slack, Discord) use a 10-second HTTP timeout. The email sender connects via SMTP with STARTTLS (port 587) or direct TLS (port 465) and a 10-second dial timeout.
   318ظْ
   319ظْ### 4.4 Delivery Flow
   320ظْ
   321ظْ```
   322ظْRiver picks up NotifyJobArgs {release_id, source_id}
   323ظْ  ظْ NotifyWorker.Work()
   324ظْ    ظْ store.GetRelease(release_id)
   325ظْ    ظْ store.ListSourceSubscriptions(source_id)
   326ظْ    ظْ for each subscription:
   327ظْ        ظْ store.GetChannel(sub.channel_id)
   328ظْ        ظْ senders[ch.type].Send(ch, notification)
   329ظْ```
   330ظْ
   331ظْ### 4.5 Error Handling
   332ظْ
   333ظْ* **Unknown channel type:** Logged as a warning and skipped. The job completes successfully (partial delivery).
   334ظْ* **Sender failure:** Individual send errors are logged but do not fail the job. Other subscriptions still receive their notifications.
   335ظْ* **Store errors:** If the release or subscription list cannot be fetched, the job returns an error and River retries per its configured retry policy.
   336ظْ* **Channel lookup failure:** If a specific channel cannot be found (e.g., deleted between subscription creation and notification), it is logged and skipped.
   337ظْ
   338ظْ## 5. Agent Architecture (ADK-Go)
   339ظْ
   340ظْ### 5.1 Overview
   341ظْ
   342ظْThe agent layer uses Google's ADK-Go (`google.golang.org/adk v0.5.0`) to create LLM-powered agents that analyze upstream releases and produce structured semantic release reports. Each agent run is scoped to a project and orchestrated through the River job queue.
   343ظْ
   344ظْ### 5.2 Agent Tools
   345ظْ
   346ظْThe agent is provided with three function tools (via ADK `functiontool`), all scoped to the project being analyzed:
   347ظْ
   348ظْ| Tool | Input | Output | Purpose |
   349ظْ|------|-------|--------|---------|
   350ظْ| `get_releases` | `{page, per_page}` | Paginated release list | Fetch recent releases for the project |
   351ظْ| `get_release_detail` | `{release_id}` | Full release with raw data | Inspect changelogs, commit lists, metadata |
   352ظْ| `list_context_sources` | `{page, per_page}` | Context source list | Discover runbooks, docs, dashboards |
   353ظْ
   354ظْTools are created by `NewTools(store, projectID)` which returns `[]tool.Tool` compatible with the ADK `llmagent.Config.Tools` field. The `toolFactory` struct holds the data store and project ID, ensuring all tool invocations are properly scoped.
   355ظْ
   356ظْ### 5.3 Store Interfaces
   357ظْ
   358ظْTwo layered interfaces separate concerns:
   359ظْ
   360ظْ```go
   361ظْ// AgentDataStore ظ¤ read-only access for tool implementations
   362ظْtype AgentDataStore interface {
   363ظْ    ListReleasesByProject(ctx, projectID, page, perPage) ([]Release, int, error)
   364ظْ    GetRelease(ctx, id) (*Release, error)
   365ظْ    ListContextSources(ctx, projectID, page, perPage) ([]ContextSource, int, error)
   366ظْ}
   367ظْ
   368ظْ// OrchestratorStore ظ¤ full access for the orchestrator lifecycle
   369ظْtype OrchestratorStore interface {
   370ظْ    AgentDataStore
   371ظْ    GetProject(ctx, id) (*Project, error)
   372ظْ    GetAgentRun(ctx, id) (*AgentRun, error)
   373ظْ    UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, id, status) error
   374ظْ    CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs) error
   375ظْ    UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, id, semanticReleaseID) error
   376ظْ    ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, projectID) ([]Subscription, error)
   377ظْ    GetChannel(ctx, id) (*NotificationChannel, error)
   378ظْ}
   379ظْ```
   380ظْ
   381ظْBoth are implemented by `api.PgStore`.
   382ظْ
   383ظْ### 5.4 Orchestrator Lifecycle
   384ظْ
   385ظْThe `Orchestrator` manages the end-to-end agent run:
   386ظْ
   387ظْ1. **Mark running** ظ¤ `UpdateAgentRunStatus(id, "running")` sets `started_at`.
   388ظْ2. **Load project** ظ¤ Fetches project config including `agent_prompt`.
   389ظْ3. **Build instruction** ظ¤ Merges the project's custom `agent_prompt` with a default system instruction that tells the agent to produce a JSON `SemanticReport`.
   390ظْ4. **Create LLM model** ظ¤ Initializes a Gemini model via `model/gemini.NewModel()` using the `GOOGLE_API_KEY` environment variable.
   391ظْ5. **Create tools** ظ¤ `NewTools(store, projectID)` creates project-scoped function tools.
   392ظْ6. **Create ADK agent** ظ¤ `llmagent.New()` wires the model, instruction, and tools into an ADK LLM agent.
   393ظْ7. **Run agent** ظ¤ Uses `runner.Runner.Run()` with an in-memory session service. Iterates over events and captures the final text response.
   394ظْ8. **Parse report** ظ¤ Attempts to parse the agent's output as a `SemanticReport` JSON. Falls back to storing raw text as the summary if parsing fails.
   395ظْ9. **Persist** ظ¤ Creates a `SemanticRelease` (with `semantic_release_sources` links) in a single transaction.
   396ظْ10. **Mark completed** ظ¤ Links the semantic release to the agent run and updates status.
   397ظْ
   398ظْ### 5.5 River Worker
   399ظْ
   400ظْThe `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`:
   401ظْ
   402ظْ```go
   403ظْfunc (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx, job) error {
   404ظْ    run := w.store.GetAgentRun(job.Args.AgentRunID)
   405ظْ    return w.orchestrator.RunAgent(ctx, run)
   406ظْ}
   407ظْ```
   408ظْ
   409ظْThe worker is registered conditionally in `main.go` ظ¤ only when `GOOGLE_API_KEY` is set. If the key is missing, agent jobs remain in the River queue unprocessed until the server is restarted with the key configured.
   410ظْ
   411ظْ### 5.6 Graceful Degradation
   412ظْ
   413ظْ* **No API key** ظ¤ `NewOrchestrator()` returns an error, the worker is not registered, and a warning is logged. The rest of the system (ingestion, notifications) continues to function.
   414ظْ* **LLM failure** ظ¤ The agent run is marked as "failed" with the error preserved in the `agent_runs` table.
   415ظْ* **Invalid agent output** ظ¤ If the LLM produces non-JSON output, the raw text is stored as the report summary rather than failing the entire run.
   416ظْ
   417ظْ## 6. Error Handling & Idempotency
   418ظْ
   419ظْ* **Idempotent Ingestion:** The `releases` table enforces a `UNIQUE(source_id, version)` constraint. If a polling worker and a webhook both report the same release from the same source simultaneously, the database rejects the duplicate, preventing duplicate notification or agent jobs.
   420ظْ* **Source-Level Filtering:** Before events enter the system, the ingestion layer applies source-level exclusion rules (`exclude_version_regexp`, `exclude_prereleases`). Filtered releases are never inserted, reducing downstream load.
   421ظْ* **Dead-Letter Queue (DLQ):** If a River job fails `max_attempts` (e.g., the LLM API is down), River marks it as `discarded`. The failure reason is captured for debugging. A monitoring hook alerts the system admin that the job requires manual intervention.
   422ظْ* **Agent Observability:** Each agent run tracks its status (`pending`, `running`, `completed`, `failed`), the prompt used, and any error encountered. If an agent run fails, the exact failure context is preserved in the `agent_runs` table for debugging.
   423ظْ* **Agent Fallback:** If the SRE Agent sandbox deployment fails due to infrastructure timeout (unrelated to the software release itself), the agent safely aborts the validation phase, flags the release event as "Unverified," and routes it to a human reviewer rather than dropping the notification.
   424ظْ* **Notification Digest Fallback:** If a digest batch fails to send (e.g., Slack API outage), the batch is retried on the next interval. Individual items within the batch are not lost ظ¤ they remain queued until the channel recovers.

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

You are a spec document reviewer. Review the design spec at /Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-16-github-oauth-login-design.md for completeness, correctness, and potential issues.

Context: This is a GitHub OAuth login feature for a Go backend + Next.js static export frontend app called Changelogue. The Go backend serves the static frontend via go:embed. Current auth is API key-based (Bearer tokens). The spec adds GitHub OAuth with server-side sessions stored in PostgreSQL and HttpOnly cookies.

Key project facts:
- Go 1.25 backend, PostgreSQL, River job queue
- Next.js static export embedded in Go binary
- Existing API key auth in internal/api/auth.go
- Migrations run at startup via internal/db/migrations.go
- NO_AUTH=true disables auth in dev

Review for:
1. Security issues (CSRF, cookie security, token handling, state validation)
2. Missing edge cases (what if both allowlists are empty? what if GitHub API fails?)
3. Schema issues (missing indexes, type choices)
4. Flow gaps (what happens on session expiry mid-use?)
5. Consistency with existing codebase patterns
6. Ambiguities that would block implementation

Format your response as:
- APPROVED (if no blocking issues)
- ISSUES FOUND (list each issue with severity: BLOCKING or SUGGESTION)

Also read the project's ARCH.md and DESIGN.md to verify consistency.

1ظْpackage main
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"context"
     5ظْ	"log/slog"
     6ظْ	"net/http"
     7ظْ	"os"
     8ظْ	"os/signal"
     9ظْ	"time"
    10ظْ
    11ظْ	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    12ظْ	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    13ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    14ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    15ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    16ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/onboard"
    17ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    18ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    19ظْ)
    20ظْ
    21ظْfunc main() {
    22ظْ	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    23ظْ	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    24ظْ	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    25ظْ	case "debug":
    26ظْ		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    27ظْ	case "warn":
    28ظْ		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    29ظْ	case "error":
    30ظْ		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    31ظْ	}
    32ظْ	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    33ظْ
    34ظْ	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
    35ظْ	defer stop()
    36ظْ
    37ظْ	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    38ظْ	addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
    39ظْ	noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
    40ظْ
    41ظْ	// Database
    42ظْ	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    43ظْ	if err != nil {
    44ظْ		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    45ظْ		os.Exit(1)
    46ظْ	}
    47ظْ	defer pool.Close()
    48ظْ
    49ظْ	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    50ظْ		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    51ظْ		os.Exit(1)
    52ظْ	}
    53ظْ
    54ظْ	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)
    55ظْ	// The river client is set to nil initially; TriggerAgentRun (which needs it)
    56ظْ	// won't be called until the server is up and the client is set below.
    57ظْ	pgStore := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    58ظْ
    59ظْ	// Register River workers
    60ظْ	workers := river.NewWorkers()
    61ظْ	notifyWorker := routing.NewNotifyWorker(pgStore, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    62ظْ	river.AddWorker(workers, notifyWorker)
    63ظْ
    64ظْ	// Agent worker: requires an LLM API key. If unavailable, agent jobs
    65ظْ	// will remain in the queue until the key is configured and the server
    66ظْ	// is restarted.
    67ظْ	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    68ظْ	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    69ظْ	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    70ظْ		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    71ظْ	}
    72ظْ	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    73ظْ		Provider:      llmProvider,
    74ظْ		Model:         envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    75ظْ		GoogleAPIKey:  os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    76ظْ		OpenAIAPIKey:  os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    77ظْ		OpenAIBaseURL: os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    78ظْ	}
    79ظْ	agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    80ظْ	if err != nil {
    81ظْ		slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available ظ¤ agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
    82ظْ	} else {
    83ظْ		agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    84ظْ		river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    85ظْ		slog.Info("agent worker registered")
    86ظْ	}
    87ظْ
    88ظْ	// Scan worker: requires LLM for dependency extraction
    89ظْ	scanScanner := onboard.NewScanner(http.DefaultClient, "", "")
    90ظْ	var scanExtractor *onboard.DependencyExtractor
    91ظْ	extractorCfg := onboard.ExtractorConfig{
    92ظْ		Provider:      llmConfig.Provider,
    93ظْ		Model:         llmConfig.Model,
    94ظْ		GoogleAPIKey:  llmConfig.GoogleAPIKey,
    95ظْ		OpenAIAPIKey:  llmConfig.OpenAIAPIKey,
    96ظْ		OpenAIBaseURL: llmConfig.OpenAIBaseURL,
    97ظْ	}
    98ظْ	ext, err := onboard.NewDependencyExtractor(ctx, extractorCfg)
    99ظْ	if err != nil {
   100ظْ		slog.Warn("dependency extractor not available", "err", err)
   101ظْ	} else {
   102ظْ		scanExtractor = ext
   103ظْ	}
   104ظْ	scanWorker := onboard.NewScanWorker(pgStore, scanScanner, scanExtractor, pool)
   105ظْ	river.AddWorker(workers, scanWorker)
   106ظْ	slog.Info("scan worker registered")
   107ظْ
   108ظْ	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
   109ظْ	if err != nil {
   110ظْ		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
   111ظْ		os.Exit(1)
   112ظْ	}
   113ظْ
   114ظْ	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
   115ظْ	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
   116ظْ	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
   117ظْ
   118ظْ	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
   119ظْ		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
   120ظْ		os.Exit(1)
   121ظْ	}
   122ظْ
   123ظْ	// Ingestion layer
   124ظْ	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   125ظْ	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   126ظْ
   127ظْ	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   128ظْ	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   129ظْ
   130ظْ	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   131ظْ
   132ظْ	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   133ظْ
   134ظْ	// Serve frontend static export if available
   135ظْ	if info, err := os.Stat("web/out"); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   136ظْ		api.RegisterFrontend(mux, "web/out")
   137ظْ		slog.Info("serving frontend from web/out")
   138ظْ	}
   139ظْ
   140ظْ	// Register all API v1 routes
   141ظْ	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   142ظْ		DB:                    pool,
   143ظْ		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   144ظْ		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   145ظْ		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   146ظْ		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   147ظْ		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   148ظْ		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   149ظْ		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   150ظْ		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   151ظْ		TodosStore:            pgStore,
   152ظْ		OnboardStore:          pgStore,
   153ظْ		PublicURL:             os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"),
   154ظْ		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   155ظْ		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   156ظْ		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   157ظْ		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   158ظْ		IngestionService:      svc,
   159ظْ		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   160ظْ	})
   161ظْ
   162ظْ	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   163ظْ
   164ظْ	// Start polling in background
   165ظْ	go orch.Run(ctx)
   166ظْ
   167ظْ	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY ظْ SSE bridge
   168ظْ	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   169ظْ
   170ظْ	// Start HTTP server
   171ظْ	go func() {
   172ظْ		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   173ظْ		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   174ظْ			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   175ظْ		}
   176ظْ	}()
   177ظْ
   178ظْ	<-ctx.Done()
   179ظْ	slog.Info("shutting down")
   180ظْ
   181ظْ	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   182ظْ	defer cancel()
   183ظْ	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   184ظْ	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   185ظْ}
   186ظْ
   187ظْfunc envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   188ظْ	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   189ظْ		return v
   190ظْ	}
   191ظْ	return fallback
   192ظْ}
   193ظْ

1ظْpackage db
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"context"
     5ظْ	"fmt"
     6ظْ
     7ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8ظْ	"github.com/riverqueue/river/riverdriver/riverpgxv5"
     9ظْ	"github.com/riverqueue/river/rivermigrate"
    10ظْ)
    11ظْ
    12ظْconst schema = `
    13ظْ-- Tracked software projects (the central entity)
    14ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
    15ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    16ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    17ظْ    description TEXT,
    18ظْ    agent_prompt TEXT,
    19ظْ    agent_rules JSONB,
    20ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    21ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    22ظْ);
    23ظْ
    24ظْ-- Configured ingestion sources (polling-based: GitHub, Docker Hub)
    25ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sources (
    26ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28ظْ    provider VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    29ظْ    repository VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    30ظْ    poll_interval_seconds INT DEFAULT 86400,
    31ظْ    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    32ظْ    config JSONB,
    33ظْ    last_polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    34ظْ    last_error TEXT,
    35ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    36ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    37ظْ    UNIQUE(provider, repository)
    38ظْ);
    39ظْ
    40ظْ-- Context sources (read-only references for agent research)
    41ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS context_sources (
    42ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    43ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    44ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    45ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    46ظْ    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    47ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    48ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    49ظْ);
    50ظْ
    51ظْ-- Source-level releases (detected from polling sources)
    52ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS releases (
    53ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    54ظْ    source_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    55ظْ    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    56ظْ    raw_data JSONB,
    57ظْ    released_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    58ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    59ظْ    UNIQUE(source_id, version)
    60ظْ);
    61ظْ
    62ظْ-- Project-level semantic releases (AI-generated, correlating source releases)
    63ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_releases (
    64ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    66ظْ    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    67ظْ    report JSONB,
    68ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    69ظْ    error TEXT,
    70ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    71ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    72ظْ    UNIQUE(project_id, version)
    73ظْ);
    74ظْ
    75ظْ-- Join table: which source releases compose a semantic release
    76ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_release_sources (
    77ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    78ظْ    release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    79ظْ    PRIMARY KEY (semantic_release_id, release_id)
    80ظْ);
    81ظْ
    82ظْ-- Notification channels (standalone)
    83ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_channels (
    84ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    85ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    86ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    87ظْ    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    88ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    89ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    90ظْ);
    91ظْ
    92ظْ-- Subscriptions: two types (source releases and semantic releases)
    93ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subscriptions (
    94ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    95ظْ    channel_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES notification_channels(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    96ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release')),
    97ظْ    source_id UUID REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    98ظْ    project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99ظْ    version_filter TEXT,
   100ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   101ظْ    CHECK (
   102ظْ        (type = 'source_release'   AND source_id  IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   103ظْ        (type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id  IS NULL)
   104ظْ    )
   105ظْ);
   106ظْ
   107ظْ-- Agent runs (scoped to project)
   108ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_runs (
   109ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   112ظْ    trigger VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   113ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   114ظْ    prompt_used TEXT,
   115ظْ    error TEXT,
   116ظْ    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   117ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   118ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   119ظْ);
   120ظْ
   121ظْALTER TABLE agent_runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version VARCHAR(100);
   122ظْ
   123ظْ-- API authentication keys
   124ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS api_keys (
   125ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   126ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   127ظْ    key_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   128ظْ    key_prefix VARCHAR(12) NOT NULL,
   129ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   130ظْ    last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   131ظْ);
   132ظْ
   133ظْ-- Trigger for SSE: notify on new releases
   134ظْCREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_release_created() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   135ظْBEGIN
   136ظْ    PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   137ظْ    RETURN NEW;
   138ظْEND;
   139ظْ$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   140ظْ
   141ظْDROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS release_created_trigger ON releases;
   142ظْCREATE TRIGGER release_created_trigger
   143ظْ    AFTER INSERT ON releases
   144ظْ    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_release_created();
   145ظْ
   146ظْ-- Trigger for SSE: notify on semantic release completion
   147ظْCREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   148ظْBEGIN
   149ظْ    IF NEW.status = 'completed' AND (OLD IS NULL OR OLD.status != 'completed') THEN
   150ظْ        PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'semantic_release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   151ظْ    END IF;
   152ظْ    RETURN NEW;
   153ظْEND;
   154ظْ$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   155ظْ
   156ظْDROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS semantic_release_trigger ON semantic_releases;
   157ظْCREATE TRIGGER semantic_release_trigger
   158ظْ    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON semantic_releases
   159ظْ    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release();
   160ظْ
   161ظْ-- Release TODOs (acknowledge/resolve tracking)
   162ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS release_todos (
   163ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   164ظْ    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   165ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   166ظْ    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   167ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   168ظْ    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   169ظْ    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   170ظْ    CHECK (
   171ظْ        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
   172ظْ        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
   173ظْ    )
   174ظْ);
   175ظْCREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_release_id ON release_todos(release_id) WHERE release_id IS NOT NULL;
   176ظْCREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_semantic_release_id ON release_todos(semantic_release_id) WHERE semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL;
   177ظْ
   178ظْ-- Onboarding scans (GitHub repo dependency detection)
   179ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS onboard_scans (
   180ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   181ظْ    repo_url TEXT NOT NULL,
   182ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   183ظْ    results JSONB,
   184ظْ    error TEXT,
   185ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   186ظْ    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   187ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   188ظْ);
   189ظْ`
   190ظْ
   191ظْ// RunMigrations applies River's schema and the application schema. Idempotent ظ¤ safe to call on every startup.
   192ظْfunc RunMigrations(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error {
   193ظْ	migrator, err := rivermigrate.New(riverpgxv5.New(pool), nil)
   194ظْ	if err != nil {
   195ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("create river migrator: %w", err)
   196ظْ	}
   197ظْ	if _, err := migrator.Migrate(ctx, rivermigrate.DirectionUp, nil); err != nil {
   198ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("river migrations: %w", err)
   199ظْ	}
   200ظْ
   201ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, schema); err != nil {
   202ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("app migrations: %w", err)
   203ظْ	}
   204ظْ
   205ظْ	// Migrate existing subscription type values: sourceظْsource_release, projectظْsemantic_release.
   206ظْ	// Drop old CHECK constraints first (both the named type check and the unnamed
   207ظْ	// composite check referencing old values), then UPDATE data, then re-add constraints.
   208ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   209ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_type_check;
   210ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_check;
   211ظْ		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'source_release' WHERE type = 'source';
   212ظْ		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'semantic_release' WHERE type = 'project';
   213ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_type_check CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release'));
   214ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_check CHECK (
   215ظْ			(type = 'source_release'   AND source_id IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   216ظْ			(type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NULL)
   217ظْ		);
   218ظْ	`); err != nil {
   219ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("subscription type migration: %w", err)
   220ظْ	}
   221ظْ
   222ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   223ظْ		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_include TEXT;
   224ظْ		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_exclude TEXT;
   225ظْ	`); err != nil {
   226ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("source version filter migration: %w", err)
   227ظْ	}
   228ظْ
   229ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   230ظْ		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS exclude_prereleases BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;
   231ظْ	`); err != nil {
   232ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("source exclude_prereleases migration: %w", err)
   233ظْ	}
   234ظْ	return nil
   235ظْ}
   236ظْ

You are a spec document reviewer. Review the design spec at /Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-16-github-oauth-login-design.md for completeness, correctness, and potential issues.

Context: This is a GitHub OAuth login feature for a Go backend + Next.js static export frontend app called Changelogue. The Go backend serves the static frontend via go:embed. Current auth is API key-based (Bearer tokens). The spec adds GitHub OAuth with server-side sessions stored in PostgreSQL and HttpOnly cookies.

Key project facts:
- Go 1.25 backend, PostgreSQL, River job queue
- Next.js static export embedded in Go binary
- Existing API key auth in internal/api/auth.go
- Migrations run at startup via internal/db/migrations.go
- NO_AUTH=true disables auth in dev

Review for:
1. Security issues (CSRF, cookie security, token handling, state validation)
2. Missing edge cases (what if both allowlists are empty? what if GitHub API fails?)
3. Schema issues (missing indexes, type choices)
4. Flow gaps (what happens on session expiry mid-use?)
5. Consistency with existing codebase patterns
6. Ambiguities that would block implementation

Format your response as:
- APPROVED (if no blocking issues)
- ISSUES FOUND (list each issue with severity: BLOCKING or SUGGESTION)

Also read the project's ARCH.md and DESIGN.md to verify consistency.

1ظْpackage api
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"net/http"
     5ظْ
     6ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9ظْ)
    10ظْ
    11ظْ// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12ظْtype Dependencies struct {
    13ظْ	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14ظْ	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15ظْ	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16ظْ	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17ظْ	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18ظْ	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19ظْ	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20ظْ	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21ظْ	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22ظْ	TodosStore            TodosStore
    23ظْ	OnboardStore          OnboardStore
    24ظْ	PublicURL             string
    25ظْ	KeyStore              KeyStore
    26ظْ	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    27ظْ	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    28ظْ	NoAuth                bool
    29ظْ	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    30ظْ	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    31ظْ}
    32ظْ
    33ظْ// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    34ظْfunc RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    35ظْ	// Public chain: no auth required.
    36ظْ	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    37ظْ
    38ظْ	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    39ظْ	chain := publicChain
    40ظْ	if !deps.NoAuth {
    41ظْ		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    42ظْ	}
    43ظْ
    44ظْ	// Projects (CRUD)
    45ظْ	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    46ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    47ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    48ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    49ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    50ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    51ظْ
    52ظْ	// Sources (nested under projects)
    53ظْ	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    54ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    55ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    56ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    57ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    58ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    59ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    60ظْ
    61ظْ	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    62ظْ	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    63ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    64ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    65ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    66ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    67ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    68ظْ
    69ظْ	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    70ظْ	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    71ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    72ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    73ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    74ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    75ظْ
    76ظْ	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    77ظْ	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    78ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    79ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    80ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    81ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    82ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    83ظْ
    84ظْ	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    85ظْ	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    86ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    87ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    88ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    89ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    90ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    91ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    92ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    93ظْ
    94ظْ	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    95ظْ	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
    96ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
    97ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
    98ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
    99ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
   100ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
   101ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
   102ظْ
   103ظْ	// Agent
   104ظْ	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   105ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   106ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   107ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   108ظْ
   109ظْ	// Todos
   110ظْ	todos := NewTodosHandler(deps.TodosStore, deps.PublicURL)
   111ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.List)))
   112ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Get)))
   113ظْ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   114ظْ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   115ظْ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/reopen", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Reopen)))
   116ظْ	// One-click endpoints for notification links (GET so they work as <a href>)
   117ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   118ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   119ظْ
   120ظْ	// Onboard (repo dependency scanning)
   121ظْ	ob := NewOnboardHandler(deps.OnboardStore)
   122ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scan", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Scan)))
   123ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.GetScan)))
   124ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Apply)))
   125ظْ
   126ظْ	// Providers (metadata ظ¤ static, no store needed)
   127ظْ	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   128ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   129ظْ
   130ظْ	// Discovery (public ظ¤ no auth, proxies external APIs)
   131ظْ	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
   132ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
   133ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))
   134ظْ
   135ظْ	// SSE events
   136ظْ	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   137ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   138ظْ
   139ظْ	// Health (public ظ¤ no auth middleware)
   140ظْ	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   141ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   142ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   143ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   144ظْ}
   145ظْ
