1ظْ.PHONY: up down db-reset build run dev test vet lint \
     2ظْ        frontend-install frontend-dev frontend-build \
     3ظْ        integration-test agent-dev clean
     4ظْ
     5ظْ# --- Configuration ---
     6ظْDATABASE_URL ?= postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable
     7ظْLISTEN_ADDR  ?= :8080
     8ظْBINARY       := changelogue
     9ظْ
    10ظْ# --- Infrastructure ---
    11ظْup:
    12ظْ	docker compose up -d
    13ظْ	@echo "Waiting for Postgres to be healthy..."
    14ظْ	@until docker compose exec -T postgres pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 1; done
    15ظْ	@echo "Postgres is ready."
    16ظْ
    17ظْdown:
    18ظْ	docker compose down
    19ظْ
    20ظْdb-reset:
    21ظْ	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS changelogue;"
    22ظْ	docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE changelogue;"
    23ظْ	@echo "Database reset."
    24ظْ
    25ظْ# --- Backend ---
    26ظْbuild:
    27ظْ	go build -o $(BINARY) ./cmd/server
    28ظْ
    29ظْrun: build
    30ظْ	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" LISTEN_ADDR="$(LISTEN_ADDR)" NO_AUTH=true ./$(BINARY)
    31ظْ
    32ظْtest:
    33ظْ	go test ./...
    34ظْ
    35ظْvet:
    36ظْ	go vet ./...
    37ظْ
    38ظْlint: vet
    39ظْ
    40ظْ# --- Agent Dev ---
    41ظْagent-dev:
    42ظْ	@if [ -z "$(PROJECT_ID)" ]; then echo "error: PROJECT_ID is required. Usage: make agent-dev PROJECT_ID=<uuid>"; exit 1; fi
    43ظْ	DATABASE_URL="$(DATABASE_URL)" go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=$(PROJECT_ID) web api webui
    44ظْ
    45ظْ# --- Frontend ---
    46ظْfrontend-install:
    47ظْ	cd web && npm install
    48ظْ
    49ظْfrontend-dev:
    50ظْ	cd web && npm run dev
    51ظْ
    52ظْfrontend-build:
    53ظْ	cd web && npm run build
    54ظْ
    55ظْ# --- Integration ---
    56ظْintegration-test:
    57ظْ	bash scripts/integration-test.sh
    58ظْ
    59ظْ# --- Convenience ---
    60ظْdev: up run
    61ظْ
    62ظْclean:
    63ظْ	rm -f $(BINARY)
    64ظْ	docker compose down -v
    65ظْ

1ظْpackage api
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"net/http"
     5ظْ
     6ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9ظْ)
    10ظْ
    11ظْ// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12ظْtype Dependencies struct {
    13ظْ	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14ظْ	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15ظْ	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16ظْ	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17ظْ	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18ظْ	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19ظْ	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20ظْ	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21ظْ	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22ظْ	TodosStore            TodosStore
    23ظْ	OnboardStore          OnboardStore
    24ظْ	PublicURL             string
    25ظْ	KeyStore              KeyStore
    26ظْ	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    27ظْ	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    28ظْ	NoAuth                bool
    29ظْ	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    30ظْ	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    31ظْ}
    32ظْ
    33ظْ// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    34ظْfunc RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    35ظْ	// Public chain: no auth required.
    36ظْ	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    37ظْ
    38ظْ	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    39ظْ	chain := publicChain
    40ظْ	if !deps.NoAuth {
    41ظْ		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    42ظْ	}
    43ظْ
    44ظْ	// Projects (CRUD)
    45ظْ	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    46ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    47ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    48ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    49ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    50ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    51ظْ
    52ظْ	// Sources (nested under projects)
    53ظْ	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)

1ظْpackage api
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"context"
     5ظْ	"crypto/sha256"
     6ظْ	"encoding/hex"
     7ظْ	"encoding/json"
     8ظْ	"errors"
     9ظْ	"fmt"
    10ظْ	"strings"
    11ظْ	"time"
    12ظْ
    13ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
    14ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    15ظْ	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    16ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    17ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    18ظْ)
    19ظْ
    20ظْ// PgStore implements all store interfaces using a PostgreSQL connection pool.
    21ظْtype PgStore struct {
    22ظْ	pool  *pgxpool.Pool
    23ظْ	river *river.Client[pgx.Tx]
    24ظْ}
    25ظْ
    26ظْ// NewPgStore returns a new PgStore backed by the given connection pool and River client.
    27ظْ// The river client may be nil if agent triggering is not needed.
    28ظْfunc NewPgStore(pool *pgxpool.Pool, riverClient *river.Client[pgx.Tx]) *PgStore {
    29ظْ	return &PgStore{pool: pool, river: riverClient}
    30ظْ}
    31ظْ
    32ظْ// SetRiverClient updates the River client on an existing PgStore.
    33ظْ// This is used during bootstrap to inject the client after worker registration.
    34ظْfunc (s *PgStore) SetRiverClient(rc *river.Client[pgx.Tx]) {
    35ظْ	s.river = rc
    36ظْ}
    37ظْ
    38ظْ// --- ProjectsStore ---
    39ظْ
    40ظْfunc (s *PgStore) ListProjects(ctx context.Context, page, perPage int) ([]models.Project, int, error) {
    41ظْ	var total int
    42ظْ	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM projects`).Scan(&total)
    43ظْ	if err != nil {
    44ظْ		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("count projects: %w", err)
    45ظْ	}
    46ظْ	offset := (page - 1) * perPage
    47ظْ	rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
    48ظْ		`SELECT id, name, COALESCE(description,''), COALESCE(agent_prompt,''),
    49ظْ		        COALESCE(agent_rules,'{}'), created_at, updated_at
    50ظْ		 FROM projects ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2`, perPage, offset)
    51ظْ	if err != nil {
    52ظْ		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("list projects: %w", err)
    53ظْ	}
    54ظْ	defer rows.Close()
    55ظْ	var projects []models.Project
    56ظْ	for rows.Next() {
    57ظْ		var p models.Project
    58ظْ		if err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.Name, &p.Description, &p.AgentPrompt, &p.AgentRules, &p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
    59ظْ			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("scan project: %w", err)
    60ظْ		}
    61ظْ		projects = append(projects, p)
    62ظْ	}
    63ظْ	return projects, total, nil
    64ظْ}
    65ظْ
    66ظْfunc (s *PgStore) CreateProject(ctx context.Context, p *models.Project) error {
    67ظْ	return s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
    68ظْ		`INSERT INTO projects (name, description, agent_prompt, agent_rules)
    69ظْ		 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)
    70ظْ		 RETURNING id, created_at, updated_at`,
    71ظْ		p.Name, p.Description, p.AgentPrompt, p.AgentRules,
    72ظْ	).Scan(&p.ID, &p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt)
    73ظْ}
    74ظْ
    75ظْfunc (s *PgStore) GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error) {
    76ظْ	var p models.Project
    77ظْ	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
    78ظْ		`SELECT id, name, COALESCE(description,''), COALESCE(agent_prompt,''),
    79ظْ		        COALESCE(agent_rules,'{}'), created_at, updated_at
    80ظْ		 FROM projects WHERE id = $1`, id,
    81ظْ	).Scan(&p.ID, &p.Name, &p.Description, &p.AgentPrompt, &p.AgentRules, &p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt)
    82ظْ	if err != nil {
    83ظْ		return nil, err
    84ظْ	}
    85ظْ	return &p, nil
    86ظْ}
    87ظْ
    88ظْfunc (s *PgStore) UpdateProject(ctx context.Context, id string, p *models.Project) error {
    89ظْ	return s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
    90ظْ		`UPDATE projects SET name=$1, description=$2, agent_prompt=$3, agent_rules=$4, updated_at=NOW()
    91ظْ		 WHERE id=$5 RETURNING updated_at`,
    92ظْ		p.Name, p.Description, p.AgentPrompt, p.AgentRules, id,
    93ظْ	).Scan(&p.UpdatedAt)
    94ظْ}
    95ظْ
    96ظْfunc (s *PgStore) DeleteProject(ctx context.Context, id string) error {
    97ظْ	tag, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, `DELETE FROM projects WHERE id = $1`, id)
    98ظْ	if err != nil {
    99ظْ		return err
   100ظْ	}

    21ظْfunc main() {

    23ظْ	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info

    35ظْ	defer stop()

    41ظْ	// Database

    43ظْ	if err != nil {
    44ظْ		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    45ظْ		os.Exit(1)
    46ظْ	}
    47ظْ	defer pool.Close()
    48ظْ
    49ظْ	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    50ظْ		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    51ظْ		os.Exit(1)
    52ظْ	}
    53ظْ
    54ظْ	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)

   102ظْ		scanExtractor = ext
   103ظْ	}
   104ظْ	scanWorker := onboard.NewScanWorker(pgStore, scanScanner, scanExtractor, pool)
   105ظْ	river.AddWorker(workers, scanWorker)
   106ظْ	slog.Info("scan worker registered")
   107ظْ
   108ظْ	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
   109ظْ	if err != nil {
   110ظْ		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
   111ظْ		os.Exit(1)
   112ظْ	}
   113ظْ
   114ظْ	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
   115ظْ	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
   116ظْ	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
   117ظْ
   118ظْ	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
   119ظْ		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
   120ظْ		os.Exit(1)
   121ظْ	}
   122ظْ
   123ظْ	// Ingestion layer
   124ظْ	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   125ظْ	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   126ظْ
   127ظْ	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   128ظْ	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   129ظْ
   130ظْ	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   131ظْ
   132ظْ	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   133ظْ
   134ظْ	// Serve frontend static export if available
   135ظْ	if info, err := os.Stat("web/out"); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   136ظْ		api.RegisterFrontend(mux, "web/out")
   137ظْ		slog.Info("serving frontend from web/out")
   138ظْ	}
   139ظْ
   140ظْ	// Register all API v1 routes
   141ظْ	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   142ظْ		DB:                    pool,
   143ظْ		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   144ظْ		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   145ظْ		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   146ظْ		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   147ظْ		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   148ظْ		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   149ظْ		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   150ظْ		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   151ظْ		TodosStore:            pgStore,
   152ظْ		OnboardStore:          pgStore,
   153ظْ		PublicURL:             os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"),
   154ظْ		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   155ظْ		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   156ظْ		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   157ظْ		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   158ظْ		IngestionService:      svc,
   159ظْ		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   160ظْ	})
   161ظْ
   162ظْ	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   163ظْ
   164ظْ	// Start polling in background
   165ظْ	go orch.Run(ctx)
   166ظْ
   167ظْ	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY ظْ SSE bridge
   168ظْ	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   169ظْ
   170ظْ	// Start HTTP server
   171ظْ	go func() {
   172ظْ		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   173ظْ		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   174ظْ			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   175ظْ		}
   176ظْ	}()
   177ظْ
   178ظْ	<-ctx.Done()
   179ظْ	slog.Info("shutting down")
   180ظْ
   181ظْ	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   182ظْ	defer cancel()
   183ظْ	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   184ظْ	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   185ظْ}
   186ظْ
   187ظْfunc envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   188ظْ	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   189ظْ		return v
   190ظْ	}
   191ظْ	return fallback
   192ظْ}

package api

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
)

// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
type Dependencies struct {
	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
	AgentStore            AgentStore
	TodosStore            TodosStore
	OnboardStore          OnboardStore
	PublicURL             string
	KeyStore              KeyStore
	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
	NoAuth                bool
	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
	HTTPClient            *http.Client
}

// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
	// Public chain: no auth required.
	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)

	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
	chain := publicChain
	if !deps.NoAuth {
		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
	}

	// Projects (CRUD)
	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))

	// Sources (nested under projects)
	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))

	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))

	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))

	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))

	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))

	// Channels (CRUD + test)
	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))

	// Agent
	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))

	// Todos
	todos := NewTodosHandler(deps.TodosStore, deps.PublicURL)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/reopen", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Reopen)))
	// One-click endpoints for notification links (GET so they work as <a href>)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))

	// Onboard (repo dependency scanning)
	ob := NewOnboardHandler(deps.OnboardStore)
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scan", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Scan)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.GetScan)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Apply)))

	// Providers (metadata ظ¤ static, no store needed)
	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))

	// Discovery (public ظ¤ no auth, proxies external APIs)
	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))

	// SSE events
	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))

	// Health (public ظ¤ no auth middleware)
	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
}

content.content:
1ظْ# GitHub OAuth Login Design
     2ظْ
     3ظْ## Overview
     4ظْ
     5ظْAdd GitHub OAuth login to Changelogue with server-side sessions, restricted to an allowlist of GitHub usernames and/or org memberships. Existing API key auth for machine clients is preserved ظ¤ both auth methods coexist.
     6ظْ
     7ظْ## OAuth Flow
     8ظْ
     9ظْ### Routes
    10ظْ
    11ظْ| Route | Auth | Purpose |
    12ظْ|-------|------|---------|
    13ظْ| `GET /auth/github` | None | Redirect to GitHub OAuth authorize URL |
    14ظْ| `GET /auth/github/callback` | None | Receive code, exchange for token, validate user, create session |
    15ظْ| `GET /auth/me` | Session | Return current user info |
    16ظْ| `POST /auth/logout` | Session | Delete session, clear cookie |
    17ظْ
    18ظْ### Flow
    19ظْ
    20ظْ1. Frontend redirects to `/auth/github`
    21ظْ2. Backend generates random `state`, stores in memory (10-min TTL, max 1000 entries), redirects to `https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize?client_id=...&state=...&scope=read:org`
    22ظْ3. GitHub redirects to `/auth/github/callback?code=...&state=...`
    23ظْ4. Backend validates `state`, exchanges `code` for access token via `https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token`
    24ظْ5. Fetches `https://api.github.com/user` and `https://api.github.com/user/orgs` using the access token
    25ظْ6. **Access token is discarded** after fetching user info ظ¤ it is not stored. Org membership is checked at login time only.
    26ظْ7. Checks username against `ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERS` and org logins against `ALLOWED_GITHUB_ORGS`
    27ظْ8. If allowed: upsert user, create session, set HttpOnly cookie, redirect to `/`
    28ظْ9. If denied: redirect to `/login?error=unauthorized`
    29ظْ
    30ظْ**Logging**: Log successful logins (`slog.Info`) and denied logins (`slog.Warn`) with username and reason.
    31ظْ
    32ظْ### Environment Variables
    33ظْ
    34ظْ| Variable | Required | Purpose |
    35ظْ|----------|----------|---------|
    36ظْ| `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID` | Prod | GitHub OAuth App client ID |
    37ظْ| `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET` | Prod | GitHub OAuth App client secret |
    38ظْ| `ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERS` | No | Comma-separated GitHub usernames |
    39ظْ| `ALLOWED_GITHUB_ORGS` | No | Comma-separated GitHub org logins |
    40ظْ| `SESSION_SECRET` | Prod | Secret for HMAC-signing session cookie values |
    41ظْ
    42ظْ**Startup validation**: If `NO_AUTH` is not `true`, the server must refuse to start if both `ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERS` and `ALLOWED_GITHUB_ORGS` are empty. Log error and `os.Exit(1)`, consistent with existing startup failure patterns.
    43ظْ
    44ظْ**Note on allowlists**: If a user changes their GitHub username after being added to `ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERS`, they lose access. This is expected ظ¤ update the allowlist accordingly.
    45ظْ
    46ظْ## Cookie Format
    47ظْ
    48ظْThe session cookie value is `sessionID.hmacSignature`:
    49ظْ- `sessionID` is the UUID from the `sessions` table
    50ظْ- `hmacSignature` is `HMAC-SHA256(sessionID, SESSION_SECRET)`, hex-encoded
    51ظْ- On validation: split cookie value at `.`, verify HMAC, then look up session in DB
    52ظْ- This prevents session ID forgery even if an attacker can guess UUIDs
    53ظْ
    54ظْCookie settings:
    55ظْ| Setting | Value |
    56ظْ|---------|-------|
    57ظْ| Name | `session` |
    58ظْ| HttpOnly | `true` |
    59ظْ| Secure | `true` by default; set `SECURE_COOKIES=false` for local dev |
    60ظْ| SameSite | `Lax` |
    61ظْ| Path | `/` |
    62ظْ
    63ظْ**CSRF note**: `SameSite=Lax` prevents cookies from being sent on cross-origin POST requests, providing CSRF protection for the logout endpoint without needing a separate CSRF token.
    64ظْ
    65ظْ## Database Schema
    66ظْ
    67ظْTwo new tables added to `internal/db/migrations.go`:
    68ظْ
    69ظْ```sql
    70ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    71ظْ    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    72ظْ    github_id       BIGINT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    73ظْ    github_login    VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    74ظْ    name            VARCHAR(200),
    75ظْ    avatar_url      TEXT,
    76ظْ    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    77ظْ    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    78ظْ);
    79ظْ
    80ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    81ظْ    id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    82ظْ    user_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83ظْ    expires_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    84ظْ    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    85ظْ);
    86ظْCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_user_id ON sessions(user_id);
    87ظْCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_expires_at ON sessions(expires_at);
    88ظْ```
    89ظْ
    90ظْ- Users are upserted on each login (updates login, name, avatar_url, updated_at)
    91ظْ- Sessions expire after 7 days, no sliding window ظ¤ user re-authenticates after expiry
    92ظْ- When a session expires mid-use, the next API call returns 401 and the frontend redirects to `/login`
    93ظْ- Deleting a user cascades to their sessions
    94ظْ
    95ظْ### Future extensibility
    96ظْ
    97ظْ- Email/password: add `email`, `password_hash` columns to `users`
    98ظْ- Google OAuth: add `google_id` column, new `/auth/google` routes
    99ظْ- Multi-provider linking: add `user_identities(user_id, provider, provider_id, email)` table
   100ظْ- Org-based access control: add `organizations` and `org_members` tables
   101ظْ- None of these require changing the session or cookie mechanism
   102ظْ
   103ظْ## Backend Package: `internal/auth/`
   104ظْ
   105ظْ| File | Responsibility |
   106ظْ|------|----------------|
   107ظْ| `oauth.go` | GitHub OAuth: authorize redirect, callback, token exchange, user info fetch |
   108ظْ| `session.go` | Session CRUD: create, validate (verify HMAC + DB lookup), delete, cleanup expired |
   109ظْ| `middleware.go` | `RequireSession` middleware: check cookie, load user, inject into context |
   110ظْ| `allowlist.go` | Parse env vars, check username/org membership, startup validation |
   111ظْ
   112ظْ### Auth middleware integration
   113ظْ
   114ظْThe existing `Auth` middleware in `internal/api/auth.go` is modified to check two auth methods in order:
   115ظْ
   116ظْ1. **Bearer token** (existing): If `Authorization: Bearer ...` header is present, validate via `KeyStore.ValidateKey()`. If invalid, return 401.
   117ظْ2. **Session cookie** (new): If no Bearer header, check for `session` cookie. Parse and verify HMAC signature, look up session in DB via a new `SessionStore` interface, load user. If invalid/expired, return 401.
   118ظْ3. If neither is present, return 401.
   119ظْ
   120ظْ**`SessionStore` interface** (implemented by `internal/auth/session.go`):
   121ظْ```go
   122ظْtype SessionStore interface {
   123ظْ    ValidateSession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*User, error)
   124ظْ}
   125ظْ```
   126ظْ
   127ظْ**Context key**: Authenticated user is stored in request context using `type contextKey string` with key `"user"`. API handlers access it via `auth.UserFromContext(ctx)` which returns `*auth.User` or nil.
   128ظْ
   129ظْ**`Auth` function signature** changes from `Auth(store KeyStore)` to `Auth(keys KeyStore, sessions SessionStore)`.
   130ظْ
   131ظْRoute auth mapping:
   132ظْ
   133ظْ```
   134ظْ/auth/github          ظْ no auth (public)
   135ظْ/auth/github/callback ظْ no auth (public)
   136ظْ/auth/me              ظْ session cookie only (uses RequireSession)
   137ظْ/auth/logout          ظْ session cookie only (uses RequireSession)
   138ظْ/api/v1/health        ظْ no auth (existing)
   139ظْ/api/v1/*             ظْ API key OR session cookie (uses modified Auth)
   140ظْ```
   141ظْ
   142ظْ## Frontend Changes
   143ظْ
   144ظْ### New files
   145ظْ
   146ظْ| File | Purpose |
   147ظْ|------|---------|
   148ظْ| `web/app/login/page.tsx` | Login page: app logo, "Sign in with GitHub" button, error display |
   149ظْ| `web/lib/auth/context.tsx` | `AuthProvider` + `useAuth()` hook |
   150ظْ
   151ظْ### Auth context
   152ظْ
   153ظْ```
   154ظْAuthProvider
   155ظْ  ظْ on mount: fetch /auth/me
   156ظْ  ظْ if 200: set user state, render children
   157ظْ  ظْ if 401 AND pathname is not /login: redirect to /login
   158ظْ  ظْ if on /login: render login page without redirect
   159ظْ  ظْ shows loading state while checking
   160ظْ```
   161ظْ
   162ظْThe `/login` page is excluded from the auth redirect loop ظ¤ `AuthProvider` checks `window.location.pathname` before redirecting.
   163ظْ
   164ظْ### Layout changes (`web/app/layout.tsx`)
   165ظْ
   166ظْ- Wrap app in `AuthProvider`
   167ظْ- Unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login` (except when already on `/login`)
   168ظْ
   169ظْ### Sidebar changes
   170ظْ
   171ظْ- Add user avatar + dropdown with "Sign out" option
   172ظْ- Sign out POSTs to `/auth/logout`, redirects to `/login`
   173ظْ
   174ظْ### No changes to API client
   175ظْ
   176ظْ`web/lib/api/client.ts` uses `fetch()` which automatically sends `HttpOnly` cookies with same-origin requests. No Authorization header needed for session-based auth.
   177ظْ
   178ظْ## Development & NO_AUTH Mode
   179ظْ
   180ظْWhen `NO_AUTH=true`:
   181ظْ- API key auth bypass continues (existing)
   182ظْ- Session auth middleware bypasses ظ¤ injects a fake dev user
   183ظْ- `/auth/me` returns a hardcoded dev user
   184ظْ- No `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID` / `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET` needed
   185ظْ- Login page accessible but not required
   186ظْ- Startup validation for allowlists is skipped
   187ظْ
   188ظْ## Session Cleanup
   189ظْ
   190ظْA background goroutine runs hourly to delete expired sessions (`expires_at < NOW()`). Starts alongside the existing polling engine in `cmd/server/main.go`. Respects context cancellation for clean shutdown, using `time.Ticker`.
   191ظْ
   192ظْ## State Parameter Storage
   193ظْ
   194ظْOAuth `state` values stored in an in-memory `sync.Map` with:
   195ظْ- 10-minute TTL per entry
   196ظْ- Maximum 1000 pending states (rejects new `/auth/github` requests with 429 if full)
   197ظْ- Cleanup goroutine sweeps expired entries every 5 minutes
   198ظْ- Sufficient for single-instance deployment; can be moved to PostgreSQL if multi-instance is needed later
   199ظْ

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

     1ظْ# GitHub OAuth Login Implementation Plan
     2ظْ
     3ظْ> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
     4ظْ
     5ظْ**Goal:** Add GitHub OAuth login with server-side sessions, restricted to an allowlist of GitHub usernames/orgs, while preserving existing API key auth for machine clients.
     6ظْ
     7ظْ**Architecture:** New `internal/auth/` package handles OAuth flow, session management, and allowlist validation. The existing `Auth` middleware in `internal/api/auth.go` is extended to accept either Bearer API keys or session cookies. Frontend gets an `AuthProvider` context that redirects unauthenticated users to a login page.
     8ظْ
     9ظْ**Tech Stack:** Go stdlib `net/http`, `crypto/hmac`, `crypto/sha256` for session signing, PostgreSQL for user/session storage, React context + fetch for frontend auth.
    10ظْ
    11ظْ**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-16-github-oauth-login-design.md`
    12ظْ
    13ظْ---
    14ظْ

    16ظْ
    17ظْ### New files (backend)
    18ظْ| File | Responsibility |
    19ظْ|------|----------------|
    20ظْ| `internal/auth/user.go` | `User` struct, context helpers (`UserFromContext`, `WithUser`) |
    21ظْ| `internal/auth/session.go` | `SessionStore` interface, session create/validate/delete/cleanup, HMAC cookie signing |
    22ظْ| `internal/auth/allowlist.go` | Parse env vars, check username/org membership, startup validation |
    23ظْ| `internal/auth/oauth.go` | GitHub OAuth handlers: authorize redirect, callback, `/auth/me`, `/auth/logout` |
    24ظْ| `internal/auth/state.go` | In-memory OAuth state parameter store with TTL and size limit |
    25ظْ| `internal/auth/session_test.go` | Tests for session HMAC signing/validation |
    26ظْ| `internal/auth/allowlist_test.go` | Tests for allowlist parsing and matching |
...

1ظْpackage api
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"net/http"
     5ظْ
     6ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9ظْ)
    10ظْ
    11ظْ// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12ظْtype Dependencies struct {
    13ظْ	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14ظْ	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15ظْ	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16ظْ	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17ظْ	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18ظْ	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19ظْ	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20ظْ	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21ظْ	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22ظْ	TodosStore            TodosStore
    23ظْ	OnboardStore          OnboardStore
    24ظْ	PublicURL             string
    25ظْ	KeyStore              KeyStore
    26ظْ	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    27ظْ	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    28ظْ	NoAuth                bool
    29ظْ	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    30ظْ	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    31ظْ}
    32ظْ
    33ظْ// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    34ظْfunc RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    35ظْ	// Public chain: no auth required.
    36ظْ	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    37ظْ
    38ظْ	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    39ظْ	chain := publicChain
    40ظْ	if !deps.NoAuth {
    41ظْ		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    42ظْ	}
    43ظْ
    44ظْ	// Projects (CRUD)
    45ظْ	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    46ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    47ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    48ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    49ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    50ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    51ظْ
    52ظْ	// Sources (nested under projects)
    53ظْ	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    54ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    55ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    56ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    57ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    58ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    59ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    60ظْ
    61ظْ	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    62ظْ	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    63ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    64ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    65ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    66ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    67ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    68ظْ
    69ظْ	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    70ظْ	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    71ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    72ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    73ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    74ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    75ظْ
    76ظْ	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    77ظْ	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    78ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    79ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    80ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    81ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    82ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    83ظْ
    84ظْ	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    85ظْ	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    86ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    87ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    88ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    89ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    90ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    91ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    92ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    93ظْ
    94ظْ	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    95ظْ	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
    96ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
    97ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
    98ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
    99ظْ	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
   100ظْ	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
   101ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
   102ظْ
   103ظْ	// Agent
   104ظْ	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   105ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   106ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   107ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   108ظْ
   109ظْ	// Todos
   110ظْ	todos := NewTodosHandler(deps.TodosStore, deps.PublicURL)
   111ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.List)))
   112ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Get)))
   113ظْ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   114ظْ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   115ظْ	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/reopen", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Reopen)))
   116ظْ	// One-click endpoints for notification links (GET so they work as <a href>)
   117ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   118ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   119ظْ
   120ظْ	// Onboard (repo dependency scanning)
   121ظْ	ob := NewOnboardHandler(deps.OnboardStore)
   122ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scan", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Scan)))
   123ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.GetScan)))
   124ظْ	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply", chain(http.HandlerFunc(ob.Apply)))
   125ظْ
   126ظْ	// Providers (metadata ظ¤ static, no store needed)
   127ظْ	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   128ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   129ظْ
   130ظْ	// Discovery (public ظ¤ no auth, proxies external APIs)
   131ظْ	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
   132ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
   133ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))
   134ظْ
   135ظْ	// SSE events
   136ظْ	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   137ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   138ظْ
   139ظْ	// Health (public ظ¤ no auth middleware)
   140ظْ	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   141ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   142ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   143ظْ	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   144ظْ}
   145ظْ

1ظْpackage main
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"context"
     5ظْ	"log/slog"
     6ظْ	"net/http"
     7ظْ	"os"
     8ظْ	"os/signal"
     9ظْ	"time"
    10ظْ
    11ظْ	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    12ظْ	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    13ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    14ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    15ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    16ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/onboard"
    17ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    18ظْ	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    19ظْ)
    20ظْ
    21ظْfunc main() {
    22ظْ	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    23ظْ	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    24ظْ	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    25ظْ	case "debug":
    26ظْ		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    27ظْ	case "warn":
    28ظْ		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    29ظْ	case "error":
    30ظْ		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    31ظْ	}
    32ظْ	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    33ظْ
    34ظْ	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
    35ظْ	defer stop()
    36ظْ
    37ظْ	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    38ظْ	addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
    39ظْ	noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
    40ظْ
    41ظْ	// Database
    42ظْ	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    43ظْ	if err != nil {
    44ظْ		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    45ظْ		os.Exit(1)
    46ظْ	}
    47ظْ	defer pool.Close()
    48ظْ
    49ظْ	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    50ظْ		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    51ظْ		os.Exit(1)
    52ظْ	}
    53ظْ
    54ظْ	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)
    55ظْ	// The river client is set to nil initially; TriggerAgentRun (which needs it)
    56ظْ	// won't be called until the server is up and the client is set below.
    57ظْ	pgStore := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    58ظْ
    59ظْ	// Register River workers
    60ظْ	workers := river.NewWorkers()
    61ظْ	notifyWorker := routing.NewNotifyWorker(pgStore, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    62ظْ	river.AddWorker(workers, notifyWorker)
    63ظْ
    64ظْ	// Agent worker: requires an LLM API key. If unavailable, agent jobs
    65ظْ	// will remain in the queue until the key is configured and the server
    66ظْ	// is restarted.
    67ظْ	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    68ظْ	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    69ظْ	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    70ظْ		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    71ظْ	}
    72ظْ	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    73ظْ		Provider:      llmProvider,
    74ظْ		Model:         envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    75ظْ		GoogleAPIKey:  os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    76ظْ		OpenAIAPIKey:  os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    77ظْ		OpenAIBaseURL: os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    78ظْ	}
    79ظْ	agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    80ظْ	if err != nil {
    81ظْ		slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available ظ¤ agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
    82ظْ	} else {
    83ظْ		agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    84ظْ		river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    85ظْ		slog.Info("agent worker registered")
    86ظْ	}
    87ظْ
    88ظْ	// Scan worker: requires LLM for dependency extraction
    89ظْ	scanScanner := onboard.NewScanner(http.DefaultClient, "", "")
    90ظْ	var scanExtractor *onboard.DependencyExtractor
    91ظْ	extractorCfg := onboard.ExtractorConfig{
    92ظْ		Provider:      llmConfig.Provider,
    93ظْ		Model:         llmConfig.Model,
    94ظْ		GoogleAPIKey:  llmConfig.GoogleAPIKey,
    95ظْ		OpenAIAPIKey:  llmConfig.OpenAIAPIKey,
    96ظْ		OpenAIBaseURL: llmConfig.OpenAIBaseURL,
    97ظْ	}
    98ظْ	ext, err := onboard.NewDependencyExtractor(ctx, extractorCfg)
    99ظْ	if err != nil {
   100ظْ		slog.Warn("dependency extractor not available", "err", err)
   101ظْ	} else {
   102ظْ		scanExtractor = ext
   103ظْ	}
   104ظْ	scanWorker := onboard.NewScanWorker(pgStore, scanScanner, scanExtractor, pool)
   105ظْ	river.AddWorker(workers, scanWorker)
   106ظْ	slog.Info("scan worker registered")
   107ظْ
   108ظْ	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
   109ظْ	if err != nil {
   110ظْ		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
   111ظْ		os.Exit(1)
   112ظْ	}
   113ظْ
   114ظْ	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
   115ظْ	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
   116ظْ	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
   117ظْ
   118ظْ	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
   119ظْ		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
   120ظْ		os.Exit(1)
   121ظْ	}
   122ظْ
   123ظْ	// Ingestion layer
   124ظْ	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   125ظْ	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   126ظْ
   127ظْ	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   128ظْ	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   129ظْ
   130ظْ	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   131ظْ
   132ظْ	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   133ظْ
   134ظْ	// Serve frontend static export if available
   135ظْ	if info, err := os.Stat("web/out"); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   136ظْ		api.RegisterFrontend(mux, "web/out")
   137ظْ		slog.Info("serving frontend from web/out")
   138ظْ	}
   139ظْ
   140ظْ	// Register all API v1 routes
   141ظْ	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   142ظْ		DB:                    pool,
   143ظْ		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   144ظْ		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   145ظْ		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   146ظْ		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   147ظْ		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   148ظْ		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   149ظْ		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   150ظْ		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   151ظْ		TodosStore:            pgStore,
   152ظْ		OnboardStore:          pgStore,
   153ظْ		PublicURL:             os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"),
   154ظْ		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   155ظْ		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   156ظْ		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   157ظْ		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   158ظْ		IngestionService:      svc,
   159ظْ		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   160ظْ	})
   161ظْ
   162ظْ	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   163ظْ
   164ظْ	// Start polling in background
   165ظْ	go orch.Run(ctx)
   166ظْ
   167ظْ	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY ظْ SSE bridge
   168ظْ	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   169ظْ
   170ظْ	// Start HTTP server
   171ظْ	go func() {
   172ظْ		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   173ظْ		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   174ظْ			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   175ظْ		}
   176ظْ	}()
   177ظْ
   178ظْ	<-ctx.Done()
   179ظْ	slog.Info("shutting down")
   180ظْ
   181ظْ	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   182ظْ	defer cancel()
   183ظْ	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   184ظْ	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   185ظْ}
   186ظْ
   187ظْfunc envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   188ظْ	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   189ظْ		return v
   190ظْ	}
   191ظْ	return fallback
   192ظْ}
   193ظْ

1ظْpackage db
     2ظْ
     3ظْimport (
     4ظْ	"context"
     5ظْ	"fmt"
     6ظْ
     7ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8ظْ	"github.com/riverqueue/river/riverdriver/riverpgxv5"
     9ظْ	"github.com/riverqueue/river/rivermigrate"
    10ظْ)
    11ظْ
    12ظْconst schema = `
    13ظْ-- Tracked software projects (the central entity)
    14ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
    15ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    16ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    17ظْ    description TEXT,
    18ظْ    agent_prompt TEXT,
    19ظْ    agent_rules JSONB,
    20ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    21ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    22ظْ);
    23ظْ
    24ظْ-- Configured ingestion sources (polling-based: GitHub, Docker Hub)
    25ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sources (
    26ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28ظْ    provider VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    29ظْ    repository VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    30ظْ    poll_interval_seconds INT DEFAULT 86400,
    31ظْ    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    32ظْ    config JSONB,
    33ظْ    last_polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    34ظْ    last_error TEXT,
    35ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    36ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    37ظْ    UNIQUE(provider, repository)
    38ظْ);
    39ظْ
    40ظْ-- Context sources (read-only references for agent research)
    41ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS context_sources (
    42ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    43ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    44ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    45ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    46ظْ    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    47ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    48ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    49ظْ);
    50ظْ
    51ظْ-- Source-level releases (detected from polling sources)
    52ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS releases (
    53ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    54ظْ    source_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    55ظْ    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    56ظْ    raw_data JSONB,
    57ظْ    released_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    58ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    59ظْ    UNIQUE(source_id, version)
    60ظْ);
    61ظْ
    62ظْ-- Project-level semantic releases (AI-generated, correlating source releases)
    63ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_releases (
    64ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    66ظْ    version VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    67ظْ    report JSONB,
    68ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    69ظْ    error TEXT,
    70ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    71ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    72ظْ    UNIQUE(project_id, version)
    73ظْ);
    74ظْ
    75ظْ-- Join table: which source releases compose a semantic release
    76ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS semantic_release_sources (
    77ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    78ظْ    release_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    79ظْ    PRIMARY KEY (semantic_release_id, release_id)
    80ظْ);
    81ظْ
    82ظْ-- Notification channels (standalone)
    83ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_channels (
    84ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    85ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    86ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    87ظْ    config JSONB NOT NULL,
    88ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    89ظْ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    90ظْ);
    91ظْ
    92ظْ-- Subscriptions: two types (source releases and semantic releases)
    93ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subscriptions (
    94ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    95ظْ    channel_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES notification_channels(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    96ظْ    type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release')),
    97ظْ    source_id UUID REFERENCES sources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    98ظْ    project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99ظْ    version_filter TEXT,
   100ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   101ظْ    CHECK (
   102ظْ        (type = 'source_release'   AND source_id  IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   103ظْ        (type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id  IS NULL)
   104ظْ    )
   105ظْ);
   106ظْ
   107ظْ-- Agent runs (scoped to project)
   108ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_runs (
   109ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   110ظْ    project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   111ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   112ظْ    trigger VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   113ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   114ظْ    prompt_used TEXT,
   115ظْ    error TEXT,
   116ظْ    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   117ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   118ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   119ظْ);
   120ظْ
   121ظْALTER TABLE agent_runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version VARCHAR(100);
   122ظْ
   123ظْ-- API authentication keys
   124ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS api_keys (
   125ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   126ظْ    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   127ظْ    key_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   128ظْ    key_prefix VARCHAR(12) NOT NULL,
   129ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   130ظْ    last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   131ظْ);
   132ظْ
   133ظْ-- Trigger for SSE: notify on new releases
   134ظْCREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_release_created() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   135ظْBEGIN
   136ظْ    PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   137ظْ    RETURN NEW;
   138ظْEND;
   139ظْ$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   140ظْ
   141ظْDROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS release_created_trigger ON releases;
   142ظْCREATE TRIGGER release_created_trigger
   143ظْ    AFTER INSERT ON releases
   144ظْ    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_release_created();
   145ظْ
   146ظْ-- Trigger for SSE: notify on semantic release completion
   147ظْCREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release() RETURNS trigger AS $$
   148ظْBEGIN
   149ظْ    IF NEW.status = 'completed' AND (OLD IS NULL OR OLD.status != 'completed') THEN
   150ظْ        PERFORM pg_notify('release_events', json_build_object('type', 'semantic_release', 'id', NEW.id)::text);
   151ظْ    END IF;
   152ظْ    RETURN NEW;
   153ظْEND;
   154ظْ$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   155ظْ
   156ظْDROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS semantic_release_trigger ON semantic_releases;
   157ظْCREATE TRIGGER semantic_release_trigger
   158ظْ    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON semantic_releases
   159ظْ    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_semantic_release();
   160ظْ
   161ظْ-- Release TODOs (acknowledge/resolve tracking)
   162ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS release_todos (
   163ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   164ظْ    release_id UUID REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   165ظْ    semantic_release_id UUID REFERENCES semantic_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   166ظْ    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   167ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   168ظْ    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   169ظْ    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   170ظْ    CHECK (
   171ظْ        (release_id IS NOT NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NULL) OR
   172ظْ        (release_id IS NULL AND semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL)
   173ظْ    )
   174ظْ);
   175ظْCREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_release_id ON release_todos(release_id) WHERE release_id IS NOT NULL;
   176ظْCREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_release_todos_semantic_release_id ON release_todos(semantic_release_id) WHERE semantic_release_id IS NOT NULL;
   177ظْ
   178ظْ-- Onboarding scans (GitHub repo dependency detection)
   179ظْCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS onboard_scans (
   180ظْ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   181ظْ    repo_url TEXT NOT NULL,
   182ظْ    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   183ظْ    results JSONB,
   184ظْ    error TEXT,
   185ظْ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   186ظْ    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   187ظْ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
   188ظْ);
   189ظْ`
   190ظْ
   191ظْ// RunMigrations applies River's schema and the application schema. Idempotent ظ¤ safe to call on every startup.
   192ظْfunc RunMigrations(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error {
   193ظْ	migrator, err := rivermigrate.New(riverpgxv5.New(pool), nil)
   194ظْ	if err != nil {
   195ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("create river migrator: %w", err)
   196ظْ	}
   197ظْ	if _, err := migrator.Migrate(ctx, rivermigrate.DirectionUp, nil); err != nil {
   198ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("river migrations: %w", err)
   199ظْ	}
   200ظْ
   201ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, schema); err != nil {
   202ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("app migrations: %w", err)
   203ظْ	}
   204ظْ
   205ظْ	// Migrate existing subscription type values: sourceظْsource_release, projectظْsemantic_release.
   206ظْ	// Drop old CHECK constraints first (both the named type check and the unnamed
   207ظْ	// composite check referencing old values), then UPDATE data, then re-add constraints.
   208ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   209ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_type_check;
   210ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS subscriptions_check;
   211ظْ		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'source_release' WHERE type = 'source';
   212ظْ		UPDATE subscriptions SET type = 'semantic_release' WHERE type = 'project';
   213ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_type_check CHECK (type IN ('source_release', 'semantic_release'));
   214ظْ		ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD CONSTRAINT subscriptions_check CHECK (
   215ظْ			(type = 'source_release'   AND source_id IS NOT NULL AND project_id IS NULL) OR
   216ظْ			(type = 'semantic_release' AND project_id IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NULL)
   217ظْ		);
   218ظْ	`); err != nil {
   219ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("subscription type migration: %w", err)
   220ظْ	}
   221ظْ
   222ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   223ظْ		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_include TEXT;
   224ظْ		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS version_filter_exclude TEXT;
   225ظْ	`); err != nil {
   226ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("source version filter migration: %w", err)
   227ظْ	}
   228ظْ
   229ظْ	if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
   230ظْ		ALTER TABLE sources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS exclude_prereleases BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;
   231ظْ	`); err != nil {
   232ظْ		return fmt.Errorf("source exclude_prereleases migration: %w", err)
   233ظْ	}
   234ظْ	return nil
   235ظْ}
   236ظْ

     4ظْ     4ظْ
     5ظْ     5ظْ**Goal:** Add GitHub OAuth login with server-side sessions, restricted to an allowlist of GitHub usernames/orgs, while preserving existing API key auth for machine clients.
     6ظْ     6ظْ
     7ظْ     7ظْ**Architecture:** New `internal/auth/` package handles OAuth flow, session management, and allowlist validation. The existing `Auth` middleware in `internal/api/auth.go` is extended to accept either Bearer API keys or session cookies. Frontend gets an `AuthProvider` context that redirects unauthenticated users to a login page.
     8ظْ     8ظْ
     9ظْ     9ظْ**Tech Stack:** Go stdlib `net/http`, `crypto/hmac`, `crypto/sha256` for session signing, PostgreSQL for user/session storage, React context + fetch for frontend auth.

   212ظْ   212ظْpackage auth

   214ظْ   214ظْimport (

   225ظْ   225ظْ)
   226ظْ   226ظْ
   227ظْ   227ظْconst sessionDuration = 7 * 24 * time.Hour
   228ظْ   228ظْ
   229ظْ   229ظْ// SessionStore manages server-side sessions in PostgreSQL.
   230ظْ   230ظْtype SessionStore struct {
   231ظْ   231ظْ	pool   *pgxpool.Pool
   232ظْ   232ظْ	secret string
   233ظْ   233ظْ}
   234ظْ   234ظْ
   235ظْ   235ظْ// NewSessionStore creates a new session store.
   236ظْ   236ظْfunc NewSessionStore(pool *pgxpool.Pool, secret string) *SessionStore {

   239ظْ   239ظْ
   240ظْ   240ظْ// SignSessionCookie produces "sessionID.hmacHex".
   241ظْ   241ظْfunc SignSessionCookie(sessionID, secret string) string {
   242ظْ   242ظْ	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
   243ظْ   243ظْ	mac.Write([]byte(sessionID))
   244ظْ   244ظْ	sig := hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
   245ظْ   245ظْ	return sessionID + "." + sig
   246ظْ   246ظْ}
   247ظْ   247ظْ
   248ظْ   248ظْ// ParseSessionCookie validates "sessionID.hmacHex" and returns the sessionID.
   249ظْ   249ظْfunc ParseSessionCookie(cookie, secret string) (string, error) {
   250ظْ   250ظْ	idx := strings.LastIndex(cookie, ".")
   251ظْ   251ظْ	if idx < 0 {
   252ظْ   252ظْ		return "", errors.New("malformed session cookie")
   253ظْ   253ظْ	}
   254ظْ   254ظْ	sessionID := cookie[:idx]
   255ظْ   255ظْ	sig := cookie[idx+1:]
   256ظْ   256ظْ
   257ظْ   257ظْ	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
   258ظْ   258ظْ	mac.Write([]byte(sessionID))
   259ظْ   259ظْ	expected := hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
   260ظْ   260ظْ
   261ظْ   261ظْ	if !hmac.Equal([]byte(sig), []byte(expected)) {
   262ظْ   262ظْ		return "", errors.New("invalid session signature")
   263ظْ   263ظْ	}
   264ظْ   264ظْ	return sessionID, nil
   265ظْ   265ظْ}
   266ظْ   266ظْ
   267ظْ   267ظْ// CreateSession creates a new session for the given user and returns the signed cookie value.
   268ظْ   268ظْfunc (s *SessionStore) CreateSession(ctx context.Context, userID string) (string, time.Time, error) {
   269ظْ   269ظْ	var sessionID string
   270ظْ   270ظْ	expiresAt := time.Now().Add(sessionDuration)
   271ظْ   271ظْ	err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
   272ظْ   272ظْ		`INSERT INTO sessions (user_id, expires_at) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING id`,
   273ظْ   273ظْ		userID, expiresAt,
   274ظْ   274ظْ	).Scan(&sessionID)

   279ظْ   279ظْ}
   280ظْ   280ظْ
   281ظْ   281ظْ// ValidateSession parses the cookie, verifies the HMAC, looks up the session, and returns the user.
   282ظْ   282ظْfunc (s *SessionStore) ValidateSession(ctx context.Context, cookie string) (*User, error) {
   283ظْ   283ظْ	sessionID, err := ParseSessionCookie(cookie, s.secret)
   284ظْ   284ظْ	if err != nil {
   285ظْ   285ظْ		return nil, err
   286ظْ   286ظْ	}
   287ظْ   287ظْ
   288ظْ   288ظْ	var u User
   289ظْ   289ظْ	err = s.pool.QueryRow(ctx, `
   290ظْ   290ظْ		SELECT u.id, u.github_id, u.github_login, COALESCE(u.name,''), COALESCE(u.avatar_url,'')
   291ظْ   291ظْ		FROM sessions s JOIN users u ON u.id = s.user_id
   292ظْ   292ظْ		WHERE s.id = $1 AND s.expires_at > NOW()
   293ظْ   293ظْ	`, sessionID).Scan(&u.ID, &u.GitHubID, &u.GitHubLogin, &u.Name, &u.AvatarURL)
   294ظْ   294ظْ	if err != nil {
   295ظْ   295ظْ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid or expired session: %w", err)
   296ظْ   296ظْ	}
   297ظْ   297ظْ	return &u, nil
   298ظْ   298ظْ}
   299ظْ   299ظْ

   743ظْ   743ظْ
   744ظْ   744ظْCreate `internal/auth/oauth.go`:
   745ظْ   745ظْ
   746ظْ   746ظْ```go
   747ظْ   747ظْpackage auth
   748ظْ   748ظْ
   749ظْ   749ظْimport (
   750ظْ   750ظْ	"context"
   751ظْ   751ظْ	"encoding/json"
   752ظْ   752ظْ	"fmt"
   753ظْ   753ظْ	"io"
   754ظْ   754ظْ	"log/slog"
   755ظْ   755ظْ	"net/http"
   756ظْ   756ظْ	"net/url"
   757ظْ   757ظْ	"time"
   758ظْ   758ظْ
   759ظْ   759ظْ	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
   760ظْ   760ظْ)
   761ظْ   761ظْ
   762ظْ   762ظْ// OAuthHandler handles GitHub OAuth flow and session endpoints.
   763ظْ   763ظْtype OAuthHandler struct {
   764ظْ   764ظْ	ClientID     string
   765ظْ   765ظْ	ClientSecret string
   766ظْ   766ظْ	Pool         *pgxpool.Pool
   767ظْ   767ظْ	Sessions     *SessionStore
   768ظْ   768ظْ	States       *StateStore
   769ظْ   769ظْ	Allowlist    *Allowlist
   770ظْ   770ظْ	SecureCookie bool
   771ظْ   771ظْ	HTTPClient   *http.Client
   772ظْ   772ظْ}
   773ظْ   773ظْ
   774ظْ   774ظْ// HandleGitHubRedirect redirects to GitHub's OAuth authorize URL.
   775ظْ   775ظْfunc (h *OAuthHandler) HandleGitHubRedirect(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   776ظْ   776ظْ	state := h.States.Create()
   777ظْ   777ظْ	if state == "" {
   778ظْ   778ظْ		http.Error(w, "too many pending login requests", http.StatusTooManyRequests)
   779ظْ   779ظْ		return
   780ظْ   780ظْ	}
   781ظْ   781ظْ
   782ظْ   782ظْ	params := url.Values{
   783ظْ   783ظْ		"client_id":    {h.ClientID},
   784ظْ   784ظْ		"state":        {state},
   785ظْ   785ظْ		"scope":        {"read:org"},
   786ظْ   786ظْ		"redirect_uri": {callbackURL(r)},
   787ظْ   787ظْ	}
   788ظْ   788ظْ	http.Redirect(w, r, "https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize?"+params.Encode(), http.StatusFound)
   789ظْ   789ظْ}
   790ظْ   790ظْ
   791ظْ   791ظْ// HandleGitHubCallback handles the OAuth callback from GitHub.
   792ظْ   792ظْfunc (h *OAuthHandler) HandleGitHubCallback(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   793ظْ   793ظْ	// Validate state
   794ظْ   794ظْ	state := r.URL.Query().Get("state")
   795ظْ   795ظْ	if !h.States.Consume(state) {
   796ظْ   796ظْ		http.Redirect(w, r, "/login?error=invalid_state", http.StatusFound)
   797ظْ   797ظْ		return
   798ظْ   798ظْ	}
   799ظْ   799ظْ
   800ظْ   800ظْ	code := r.URL.Query().Get("code")
   801ظْ   801ظْ	if code == "" {
   802ظْ   802ظْ		http.Redirect(w, r, "/login?error=missing_code", http.StatusFound)
   803ظْ   803ظْ		return
   804ظْ   804ظْ	}
   805ظْ   805ظْ
   806ظْ   806ظْ	client := h.HTTPClient
   807ظْ   807ظْ	if client == nil {
   808ظْ   808ظْ		client = http.DefaultClient
   809ظْ   809ظْ	}
   810ظْ   810ظْ
   811ظْ   811ظْ	// Exchange code for access token
   812ظْ   812ظْ	token, err := exchangeCode(client, h.ClientID, h.ClientSecret, code)
   813ظْ   813ظْ	if err != nil {
   814ظْ   814ظْ		slog.Error("github token exchange failed", "err", err)
   815ظْ   815ظْ		http.Redirect(w, r, "/login?error=token_exchange", http.StatusFound)
   816ظْ   816ظْ		return
   817ظْ   817ظْ	}
   818ظْ   818ظْ
   819ظْ   819ظْ	// Fetch user info
   820ظْ   820ظْ	ghUser, err := fetchGitHubUser(client, token)
   821ظْ   821ظْ	if err != nil {
   822ظْ   822ظْ		slog.Error("github user fetch failed", "err", err)
   823ظْ   823ظْ		http.Redirect(w, r, "/login?error=user_fetch", http.StatusFound)
   824ظْ   824ظْ		return
   825ظْ   825ظْ	}
   826ظْ   826ظْ
   827ظْ   827ظْ	// Fetch user orgs
   828ظْ   828ظْ	orgs, err := fetchGitHubOrgs(client, token)
   829ظْ   829ظْ	if err != nil {
   830ظْ   830ظْ		slog.Warn("github orgs fetch failed, proceeding without orgs", "err", err)
   831ظْ   831ظْ	}
   832ظْ   832ظْ
   833ظْ   833ظْ	// Check allowlist
   834ظْ   834ظْ	orgLogins := make([]string, len(orgs))
   835ظْ   835ظْ	for i, o := range orgs {
   836ظْ   836ظْ		orgLogins[i] = o.Login
   837ظْ   837ظْ	}
   838ظْ   838ظْ	if !h.Allowlist.IsUserAllowed(ghUser.Login, orgLogins) {
   839ظْ   839ظْ		slog.Warn("login denied: user not in allowlist", "login", ghUser.Login, "orgs", orgLogins)
   840ظْ   840ظْ		http.Redirect(w, r, "/login?error=unauthorized", http.StatusFound)
   841ظْ   841ظْ		return
   842ظْ   842ظْ	}
   843ظْ   843ظْ
   844ظْ   844ظْ	// Upsert user
   845ظْ   845ظْ	var userID string
   846ظْ   846ظْ	err = h.Pool.QueryRow(r.Context(), `
   847ظْ   847ظْ		INSERT INTO users (github_id, github_login, name, avatar_url)
   848ظْ   848ظْ		VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)
   849ظْ   849ظْ		ON CONFLICT (github_id) DO UPDATE SET
   850ظْ   850ظْ			github_login = EXCLUDED.github_login,
   851ظْ   851ظْ			name = EXCLUDED.name,
   852ظْ   852ظْ			avatar_url = EXCLUDED.avatar_url,
   853ظْ   853ظْ			updated_at = NOW()
   854ظْ   854ظْ		RETURNING id
   855ظْ   855ظْ	`, ghUser.ID, ghUser.Login, ghUser.Name, ghUser.AvatarURL).Scan(&userID)
